meta.name: 2 Esdras

text.KJV: 1:1 The second booke of the Prophet Esdras the sonne of Saraias, the sonne of Azarias, the sonne of Helchias, the sonne of Sadamias, the sonne of Sadoc, the sonne of Achitob,
text.NRSV: The book of the prophet Ezra son of Seraiah, son of Azariah, son of Hilkiah, son of Shallum, son of Zadok, son of Ahitub,
text.DRB: The second Book of Esdras the Prophet, the son of Sarei, the son of Azarei, the son of Helcias, the son of Sadanias, the son of Sadoch, the son of Achitob

text.KJV: 1:2 The sonne of Achias, the sonne of Phinees, the sonne of Heli, the sonne of Amarias, the sonne of Aziei, the sonne of Marimoth, the sonne of Arna, the sonne of Ozias, the sonne of Borith, the sonne of Abisei, the sonne of Phinees, the sonne of Eleazar,
text.NRSV: son of Ahijah, son of Phinehas, son of Eli, son of Amariah, son of Azariah, son of Meraioth, son of Arna, son of Uzzi, son of Borith, son of Abishua, son of Phinehas, son of Eleazar,
text.DRB: the son of Achias, the son of Phinees, the son of Heli, the son of Amerias, the son of Asiel, the son of Marimoth, the son of Arna, the son of Ozias, the son of Borith, the son of Abisei, the son of Phinees, the son of Eleazar,

text.KJV: 1:3 The sonne of Aaron, of the Tribe of Leui, which was captiue in the land of the Medes, in the reigne of Artaxerxes king of the Persians.
text.NRSV: son of Aaron, of the tribe of Levi, who was a captive in the country of the Medes in the reign of Artaxerxes, king of the Persians.
text.DRB: the son of Aaron of the tribe of Levi; who was captive in the country of the Medes, in the reign of Artaxerxes king of the Persians.

text.KJV: 1:4 And the word of the Lord came vnto me, saying,
text.NRSV: The word of the Lord came to me, saying,
text.DRB: And the word of our Lord came to me, saying:

text.KJV: 1:5 Goe thy way, and shew my people their sinfull deeds, and their children their wickednes which they haue done against me, that they may tell their childrens children,
text.NRSV: “Go, declare to my people their evil deeds, and to their children the iniquities that they have committed against me, so that they may tell their children’s children.”
text.DRB: Go, and tell my people their wicked deeds, and their children the iniquities, that they have done against me, that they tell their children's children:
because the sins of their parents are increased in them, for they being forgetful of me have sacrificed to strange gods.
text.KJV: 1:6 Because the sinnes of their fathers are increased in them: for they haue forgotten me, & haue offered vnto strange gods.
text.NRSV: that the sins of their parents have increased in them, for they have forgotten me and have offered sacrifices to strange gods.

text.DRB: Did not I bring them out of the land of Ægypt from the house of bondage? But they have provoked me, and have despised my counsels.
text.KJV: 1:7 Am not I euen hee that brought them out of the land of Egypt, from the house of bondage? but they haue prouoked me vnto wrath, and despised my counsels.
text.NRSV: Was it not I who brought them out of the land of Egypt, out of the house of bondage? But they have angered me and despised my counsels.

text.DRB: But do thou shake off the hair of thy head, & throw all evils upon them: because they have not obeyed my law. And it is a people without discipline.
text.KJV: 1:8 Pull thou off then the haire of thy head, and cast all euill vpon them, for they haue not beene obedient vnto my law, but it is a rebellious people.
text.NRSV: Now you, pull out the hair of your head and hurl] all evils upon them, for they have not obeyed my law—they are a rebellious people.

text.DRB: How long shall I bear with them, on whom I have bestowed so great benefits?
text.KJV: 1:9 How long shall I forbeare them vnto whō I haue done so much good?
text.NRSV: How long shall I endure them, on whom I have bestowed such great benefits?

text.DRB: I have overthrown many kings from them. I have struck Pharao with his servants, and all his host. Exod. 14.
text.KJV: 1:10 Many kings haue I destroyed for their sakes, Pharao with his seruants, and all his power haue I smitten downe.
text.NRSV: For their sake I have overthrown many kings; I struck down Pharaoh with his servants and all his army.

text.DRB: All nations did I destroy before their face, & in the East I dissipated the peoples of two provinces Tyre and Sidon, and I slew all their adversaries.
text.KJV: 1:11 All the nations haue I destroyed before them, & in the East I haue scattered the people of two prouinces, euen of Tyrus and Sidon, and haue slaine all their enemies.
text.NRSV: I destroyed all nations before them, and scattered in the east the peoples of two provinces,] Tyre and Sidon; I killed all their enemies.

text.DRB: But speak thou to them, saying: Thus saith our Lord:
text.KJV: 1:12 Speake thou therefore vnto them saying, Thus saith the Lord,
text.NRSV: ‘But speak to them and say, Thus says the Lord:

text.DRB: I made you pass through the sea, and gave you fenced streets from the beginning. I gave you Moyses for your governor, and Aaron for the Priest:
text.KJV: 1:13 I led you through the Sea, and in the beginning gaue you a large and safe passage, I gaue you Moyses for a leader, and Aaron for a priest,
text.NRSV: Surely it was I who brought you through the sea, and made safe highways for you where there was no road; I gave you Moses as leader and Aaron as priest;

text.DRB: I gave you light by the pillar of fire, and did many marvelous things among you: but you have forgotten me, saith our Lord. Exod. 13.
text.KJV: 1:14 I gaue you light in a pillar of fire, and great wonders haue I done among you, yet haue you forgotten me, saith the Lord.
text.NRSV: I provided light for you from a pillar of fire, and did great wonders among you. Yet you have forgotten me, says the Lord.

text.DRB: Thus saith our Lord omnipotent: The quail was a sign to you, I gave you a camp for defence, & there you murmured: Exod. 16.
text.KJV: 1:15 Thus saith the Almightie Lord, The quailes were as a token for you, I gaue you tents for your safegard, neuerthelesse you murmured there,
text.NRSV: ‘Thus says the Lord Almighty:] The quails were a sign to you; I gave you camps for your protection, and in them you complained.

text.DRB: And you triumphed not in my name for the destruction of your enemies, but yet until now you have murmured.
text.KJV: 1:16 And triumphed not in my name for the destruction of your enemies, but euer to this day doe ye yet murmure.
text.NRSV: You have not exulted in my name at the destruction of your enemies, but to this day you still complain.]

text.DRB: Where are the benefits that I have given you? Did you not cry out to me when you were hungry in the desert, .
text.KJV: 1:17 Where are the benefits that I haue done for you? when you were hungry and thirstie in the wildernesse, did you not crie vnto me?
text.NRSV: Where are the benefits that I bestowed on you? When you were hungry and thirsty in the wilderness, did you not cry out to me,

text.DRB: saying: Why hast thou brought us into this desert to kill us, it had been better for us to serve the Ægyptians, than to die in this desert.
text.KJV: 1:18 Saying, Why hast thou brought vs into this wildernesse to kill vs? It had bin better for vs to haue serued the Egyptians, then to die in this wildernesse.
text.NRSV: saying, “Why have you led us into this wilderness to kill us? It would have been better for us to serve the Egyptians than to die in this wilderness.”

text.DRB: I was sorry for your mournings, & gave you manna to eat. You did eat bread of Angels. Exod. 16. . . v.
text.KJV: 1:19 Then had I pity vpon your mournings, and gaue you Manna to eat, so ye did eate Angels bread.
text.NRSV: I pitied your groanings and gave you manna for food; you ate the bread of angels.

text.DRB: When you thirsted did not I cleave the rock, & waters flowed in aboundance? For the heats I covered you with the leaves of trees.
text.KJV: 1:20 When ye were thirstie, did I not cleaue the rocke, and waters flowed out to your fill? for the heate I couered you with the leaues of the trees.
text.NRSV: When you were thirsty, did I not split the rock so that waters flowed in abundance? Because of the heat I clothed you with the leaves of trees.]

text.DRB: I delivered unto you fat lands: The Chananeites, and Pherezeites, & Philistheans I threw out from your face: what shall I
text.KJV: 1:21 I diuided amongst you a fruitfull land, I cast out the Canaanites, the Pherezites, and the Philistines before you: what shall I yet doe more for you: saith the Lord?
text.NRSV: I divided fertile lands among you; I drove out the Canaanites, the Perizzites, and the Philistines] before you. What more can I do for you? says the Lord.

text.DRB: Thus saith our Lord omnipotent: In the desert when you were thirsty in the river of the Amorrheites, and blaspheming my name
text.KJV: 1:22 Thus saith the Almighty Lord, when you were in the wildernes in the riuer of the Amorites, being athirst, and blaspheming my Name,
text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord Almighty:] When you were in the wilderness, at the bitter stream, thirsty and blaspheming my name,

text.DRB: I gave you not fire for blasphemies, but casting wood into the water, I made the river sweet. .
text.KJV: 1:23 I gaue you not fire for your blasphemies, but cast a tree in the water, and made the riuer sweet.
text.NRSV: I did not send fire on you for your blasphemies, but threw a tree into the water and made the stream sweet.

text.DRB: What shall I do to thee Jacob? Thou wouldest not obey o Juda. I will transfer myself to other nations, & will give them my name, that they may keep my ordinances.
text.KJV: 1:24 What shall I doe vnto thee, O Iacob? thou Iuda wouldest not obey me: I will turne me to other nations, and vnto those will I giue my Name, that they may keepe my Statutes.
text.NRSV: ‘What shall I do to you, O Jacob? You, Judah, would not obey me. I will turn to other nations and will give them my name, so that they may keep my statutes.

text.DRB: Because you have forsaken me, I also forsake you: when you ask mercy of me, I will not have mercy.
text.KJV: 1:25 Seeing yee haue forsaken mee, I will forsake you also: when yee desire me to be gracious vnto you, I shall haue no mercy vpon you.
text.NRSV: Because you have forsaken me, I also will forsake you. When you beg mercy of me, I will show you no mercy.

text.DRB: When you shall invocate me, I will not hear you. For you have defiled your hands with blood, & your feet are quick to commit murders.
text.KJV: 1:26 Whensoeuer you shall call vpon me, I will not heare you: for yee haue defiled your hands with blood, and your feete are swift to commit manslaughter.
text.NRSV: When you call to me, I will not listen to you; for you have defiled your hands with blood, and your feet are swift to commit murder.

text.DRB: Not as though you have forsaken me, but yourselves, saith our Lord.
text.KJV: 1:27 Yee haue not as it were forsaken me, but your owne selues, saith the Lord.
text.NRSV: It is not as though you had forsaken me; you have forsaken yourselves, says the Lord.

text.DRB: Thus saith our Lord omnipotent, have not I desired you, as a father his sons, & a mother her daughters, & as a nurse her little ones,
text.KJV: 1:28 Thus saith the Almighty Lord, Haue I not prayed you as a father his sonnes, as a mother her daughters, and a nurse her young babes,
text.NRSV: ‘Thus says the Lord Almighty: Have I not entreated you as a father entreats his sons or a mother her daughters or a nurse her children,

text.DRB: that you would be my people, and I your God, and to me for children, and I to you for a father?
text.KJV: 1:29 That yee would be my people, and I shoud be your God, that ye would be my children, and I should be your father?
text.NRSV: so that you should be my people and I should be your God, and that you should be my children and I should be your father?

text.KJV: 1:30 I gathered you together, as a henne gathereth her chickens vnder her wings: but now, what shall I doe vnto you? I will cast you out from my face.
text.NRSV: “I gathered you as a hen gathers her chicks under her wings. But now, what shall I do to you? I will cast you out from my presence.”
text.DRB: So have I gathered you, as the hen her chickens under her wings. But now what shall I do to you? I will throw you from my face. . v.
When you shall bring me oblation, I will turn away my face from you. For I have refused your festival days, & new moons & circumcisions.
text.KJV: 1:31 When you offer vnto me, I will turne my face from you: for your solemne feast dayes, your newe Moone, and your circumcisions haue I forsaken.
text.NRSV: When you offer oblations to me, I will turn my face from you; for I have rejected your] festal days, and new moons, and circumcisions of the flesh.]

text.DRB: I sent my servants the Prophets to you, whom being taken you slew, & mangled their bodies, whose blood I will require, saith our Lord.
text.KJV: 1:32 I sent vnto you my seruants the Prophets, whom yee haue taken and slaine, and torne their bodies in pieces, whose blood I will require of your hands, saith the Lord.
text.NRSV: I sent you my servants the prophets, but you have taken and killed them and torn their bodies] in pieces; I will require their blood of you, says the Lord.]

text.DRB: Thus saith our Lord omnipotent, your house is made desolate, I will throw you away, as the wind doth stubble,
text.KJV: 1:33 Thus saith the Almighty Lord, Your house is desolate, I will cast you out, as the wind doth stubble.
text.NRSV: ‘Thus says the Lord Almighty: Your house is desolate; I will drive you out as the wind drives straw;

text.DRB: & your children shall not have issue: because they have neglected my commandment, & have done that which is evil before me.
text.KJV: 1:34 And your children shall not bee fruitful: for they haue despised my Commandement, and done the thing that is euill before me.
text.NRSV: and your sons will have no children, because with you] they have neglected my commandment and have done what is evil in my sight.

text.DRB: I will deliver your houses to a people coming, who not hearing me do believe: to whom I have not shewed signs, they will do the things that I have commanded.
text.KJV: 1:35 Your houses wil I giue to a people that shall come, which not hauing heard of mee, yet shall beleeue mee, to whom I haue shewed no signes, yet they shall doe that I haue commaunded them.
text.NRSV: I will give your houses to a people that will come, who without having heard me will believe. Those to whom I have shown no signs will do what I have commanded.

text.DRB: The Prophets they have not seen, & they will be mindful of their iniquities.
text.KJV: 1:36 They haue seene no Prophets, yet they shall call their sinnes to remembrance, and acknowledge them.
text.NRSV: They have seen no prophets, yet will recall their former state.]

text.KJV: 1:37 I take to witnesse the grace of the people to come, whose little ones reioyce in gladnesse: and though they haue not seene me with bodily eyes, yet in spirit they beleeue the thing that I say.
text.NRSV: “I call to witness the gratitude of the people that is to come, whose children rejoice with gladness; though they do not see me with bodily eyes, yet with the spirit they believe the things that I have said.”
text.DRB: I call to witness the grace of the people coming, whose little ones rejoice with joy, not seeing me with their carnal eyes, but in spirit believing the things that I have said.
And now brother behold what glory: and see people coming from the east,
text.KJV: 1:38 And now brother, behold what glory: and see the people that commeth from the East.
text.NRSV: ‘And now, father,] look with pride and see the people coming from the east;

text.DRB: to whom I will give the conduction of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, and of Osee, & Amos, and of Joel, & Abdias, & Jonas, & Michæas,
text.KJV: 1:39 Unto whom I will giue for leaders, Abraham, Isaac, and Iacob, Oseas, Amos, and Micheas, Ioel, Abdias, and Ionas,
text.NRSV: to them I will give as leaders Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, and Hosea and Amos and Micah and Joel and Obadiah and Jonah

text.DRB: & Naum & Habacuc, of Sophonias, Aggæus, Zacharias, & Malachias. Who also is called the Angel of our Lord.
text.KJV: 1:40 Nahum, and Abacuc, Sophonias, Aggeus, Zacharie, and Malachie, which is called also an Angel of the Lord.
text.NRSV: and Nahum and Habakkuk, Zephaniah, Haggai, Zechariah, and Malachi, who is also called the messenger of the Lord.]

text.KJV: 2:1 Thus saith the Lord, I brought this people out of bondage, and I gaue them my Commaundements by my seruants the prophets, whom they would not heare, but despised my counsailes.
text.NRSV: “Thus says the Lord: I brought this people out of bondage, and I gave them commandments through my servants the prophets; but they would not listen to them, and made my counsels void.”

text.DRB: Their mother that bare them, saith to them: Go children, because I am a widow & forsaken.
text.KJV: 2:2 The mother that bare them, saith vnto them, Goe your way ye children, for I am a widow, and forsaken.
text.NRSV: The mother who bore them] says to them, “Go, my children, because I am a widow and forsaken.

text.DRB: I brought you up with joy, and have lost you with mourning and sorrow, because you have sinned before our Lord your God, & have done that which is evil before him.
text.KJV: 2:3 I brought you vp with gladnesse, but with sorrow and heauinesse haue I lost you: for yee haue sinned before the Lord your God, and done that thing that is euil before him.
text.NRSV: I brought you up with gladness; but with mourning and sorrow I have lost you, because you have sinned before the Lord God and have done what is evil in my sight.]

text.DRB: But now what shall I do to you? I am a widow and desolate, go my children, and ask mercy of our Lord.
text.KJV: 2:4 But what shall I now doe vnto you? I am a widow and forsaken: goe your way, O my children, and aske mercy of the Lord.
text.NRSV: But now what can I do for you? For I am a widow and forsaken. Go, my children, and ask for mercy from the Lord.”

text.DRB: And I call thee o father a witness upon the mother of the children, that would not keep my testament,
text.KJV: 2:5 As for mee, O father, I call vpon thee for a witnesse ouer the mother of these children, which would not keepe my Couenant,
text.NRSV: Now I call upon you, father, as a witness in addition to the mother of the children, because they would not keep my covenant,

text.DRB: that thou give them confusion, and their mother into spoil, that there be no generation of them.
text.KJV: 2:6 That thou bring them to confusion, and their mother to a spoile, that there may be no off spring of them.
text.NRSV: so that you may bring confusion on them and bring their mother to ruin, so that they may have no offspring.

text.DRB: Let their names be dispersed into the Gentiles, let them be destroyed out of the land: because they have despised my sacrament.
text.KJV: 2:7 Let them bee scattered abroad among the heathen, let their names bee put out of the earth: for they haue despised my Couenant.
text.NRSV: Let them be scattered among the nations; let their names be blotted out from the earth, because they have despised my covenant.

text.DRB: Woe be to thee Assur, which hidest the wicked with thee. Thou naughty nation, remember what I did to Sodom and Gomorrha:
text.KJV: 2:8 Woe be vnto thee Assur, thou that hidest the vnrighteous in thee, O thou wicked people, remember what I did vnto Sodome and Gomorrhe.
text.NRSV: ‘Woe to you, Assyria, who conceal the unrighteous within you! O wicked nation, remember what I did to Sodom and Gomorrah,

text.DRB: whose land lieth in clods of pitch, and heaps of ashes: so will I make them, that have not heard me, saith our Lord omnipotent.
text.KJV: 2:9 Whose land lieth in clods of pitch and heapes of ashes: euen so also wil I doe vnto them that heare me not, saith the Almightie Lord.
text.NRSV: whose land lies in lumps of pitch and heaps of ashes.] That is what I will do to those who have not listened to me, says the Lord Almighty.’

text.KJV: 2:10 Thus saith the Lord vnto Esdras, Tell my people that I will giue them the kingdome of Hierusalem, which I would haue giuen vnto Israel.
text.NRSV: “Thus says the Lord to Ezra: Tell my people that I will give them the kingdom of Jerusalem, which I was going to give to Israel.”
text.DRB: Thus saith our Lord to Esdras: Tell my people, that I will give them the Kingdom of Jerusalem, which I meant to give to Israel.
And I will take to me the glory of them, and will give them eternal tabernacles, which I had prepared for them.
text.KJV: 2:11 Their glory also wil I take vnto mee, and giue these the euerlasting Tabernacles, which I had prepared for them.
text.NRSV: Moreover, I will take back to myself their glory, and will give to these others the everlasting habitations, which I had prepared for Israel.]

text.DRB: The wood of life shall be to them for an odour of ointment, & they shall not labour, nor be wearied.
text.KJV: 2:12 They shall haue the tree of Life for an oyntment of sweet sauour, they shall nether labour, nor be weary.
text.NRSV: The tree of life shall give them fragrant perfume, and they shall neither toil nor become weary.

text.DRB: Go and you shall receive. Ask for yourselves a few days, that they may abide. Now the Kingdom is prepared for you, watch ye.
text.KJV: 2:13 Goe and yee shall receiue: pray for few dayes vnto you, that they may be shortned: the kingdome is already prepared for you: Watch.
text.NRSV: Go,] and you will receive; pray that your days may be few, that they may be shortened. The kingdom is already prepared for you; be on the watch!

text.DRB: Call thou heaven and earth to witness: for I have destroyed evil, and have created good, because I live saith our Lord.
text.KJV: 2:14 Take heauen and earth to witnesse; for I haue broken the euill in pieces, and created the good; for I liue, saith the Lord.
text.NRSV: Call, O call heaven and earth to witness: I set aside evil and created good; for I am the Living One, says the Lord.

text.DRB: Mother embrace thy children, bring them up with joy. As a dove confirm their feet: because I have chosen thee, saith our Lord.
text.KJV: 2:15 Mother, embrace thy children, and bring them vp with gladnesse, make their feet as fast as a pillar: for I haue chosen thee, saith the Lord.
text.NRSV: ‘Mother, embrace your children; bring them up with gladness, as does a dove; strengthen their feet, because I have chosen you, says the Lord.

text.DRB: And I will raise again the dead out of their places, and out of the monuments I will bring them forth, because I have known my name in Israel.
text.KJV: 2:16 And those that be dead wil I raise vp againe from their places, and bring them out of the graues: for I haue knowen my Name in Israel.
text.NRSV: And I will raise up the dead from their places, and bring them out from their tombs, because I recognize my name in them.

text.DRB: Fear not o mother of the children, because I have chosen thee, saith our Lord.
text.KJV: 2:17 Feare not thou mother of the children: for I haue chosen thee, saith the Lord.
text.NRSV: Do not fear, mother of children, for I have chosen you, says the Lord.

text.DRB: I will send thee aid, my servants Isaie, and Jeremie, at whose counsel I have sanctified, and prepared for thee twelve trees loden with diverse fruits.
text.KJV: 2:18 For thy helpe I will send my seruants Esay and Ieremie, after whose counsaile I haue sanctified and prepared for thee twelue trees, laden with diuers fruits;
text.NRSV: I will send you help, my servants Isaiah and Jeremiah. According to their counsel I have consecrated and prepared for you twelve trees loaded with various fruits,

text.DRB: & as many fountains flowing milk and honey: and seven huge mountains, having the rose and the lily, in the which I will fill thy children with joy. Exod. 15. v. 27.
text.KJV: 2:19 And as many fountaines flowing with milke and hony: and seuen mightie mountaines, whereupon there grow roses and lillies, whereby I will fill thy children with ioy.
text.NRSV: and the same number of springs flowing with milk and honey, and seven mighty mountains on which roses and lilies grow; by these I will fill your children with joy.

text.DRB: Justify thou the widow, judge for the pupil, give to the needy, defend the orphan, clothe the naked,
text.KJV: 2:20 Doe right to the widow, iudge for the fatherlesse, giue to the poore, defend the orphane, clothe the naked,
text.NRSV: ‘Guard the rights of the widow, secure justice for the ward, give to the needy, defend the orphan, clothe the naked,

text.DRB: cure the broken and feeble, mock not the lame, defend the maimed, and admit the blind to the vision of my glory.
text.KJV: 2:21 Heale the broken and the weake, laugh not a lame man to scorne, defend the maimed, and let the blind man come into the sight of my clearenesse.
text.NRSV: care for the injured and the weak, do not ridicule the lame, protect the maimed, and let the blind have a vision of my splendour.

text.DRB: The old man & the young keep within thy walls:
text.KJV: 2:22 Keepe the olde and yong within thy walles.
text.NRSV: Protect the old and the young within your walls.

text.DRB: where thou shalt find the dead, commit them to the grave signing it, and I will give thee the first seat in my resurrection.
text.KJV: 2:23 Wheresouer thou findest the dead, take them and bury them, and I will giue thee the first place in my resurrection.
text.NRSV: When you find any who are dead, commit them to the grave and mark it,] and I will give you the first place in my resurrection.

text.DRB: Pause and rest my people, because thy rest shall come.
text.KJV: 2:24 Abide still, O my people, and take thy rest, for thy quietnesse shall come.
text.NRSV: Pause and be quiet, my people, because your rest will come.

text.DRB: As a good nurse nourish thy children, confirm their feet.
text.KJV: 2:25 Nourish thy children, O thou good nource, stablish their feete.
text.NRSV: ‘Good nurse, nourish your children; strengthen their feet.

text.DRB: The servants that I have given thee, none of them shall perish. For I will require them of thy number.
text.KJV: 2:26 As for the seruants whom I haue giuen thee, there shall not one of them perish; for I will require them from among thy number.
text.NRSV: Not one of the servants] whom I have given you will perish, for I will require them from among your number.

text.DRB: Be not wearied. For when the day of affliction and distress shall come, others shall weep, & be sad, but thou shalt be merry and plenteous.
text.KJV: 2:27 Be not weary, for when the day of trouble and heauinesse commeth, others shal weepe and be sorrowfull, but thou shalt be merry, and haue abundance.
text.NRSV: Do not be anxious, for when the day of tribulation and anguish comes, others shall weep and be sorrowful, but you shall rejoice and have abundance.

text.DRB: The gentiles shall envy, & shall be able to do nothing against thee, saith our Lord.
text.KJV: 2:28 The heathen shall enuie thee, but they shall be able to doe nothing against thee, sayth the Lord.
text.NRSV: The nations shall envy you, but they shall not be able to do anything against you, says the Lord.

text.DRB: My hands shall cover thee, that thy children see not hell.
text.KJV: 2:29 My hands shal couer thee, so that thy children shall not see hell.
text.NRSV: My power will protect] you, so that your children may not see hell.]

text.DRB: Be pleasant thou mother with thy children, because I will deliver thee saith our Lord.
text.KJV: 2:30 Be ioyfull, O thou mother, with thy children, for I will deliuer thee, sayth the Lord.
text.NRSV: ‘Rejoice, O mother, with your children, because I will deliver you, says the Lord.

text.DRB: Remember thy children that sleep, for I will bring them out of the sides of the earth, & will do mercy with them: because I am merciful, saith our Lord omnipotent.
text.KJV: 2:31 Remember thy children that sleep, for I shall bring them out of the sides of the earth, and shew mercy vnto them: for I am mercifull, sayth the Lord Almightie.
text.NRSV: Remember your children that sleep, because I will bring them out of the hiding-places of the earth, and will show mercy to them; for I am merciful, says the Lord Almighty.

text.DRB: Embrace thy children till I come, & shew them mercy: because my fountains run over, & my grace shall not fail.
text.KJV: 2:32 Embrace thy children vntill I come and shew mercy vnto them: for my welles runne ouer, and my grace shall not faile.
text.NRSV: Embrace your children until I come, and proclaim mercy to them; because my springs run over, and my grace will not fail.’

text.KJV: 2:33 I Esdras receiued a charge of the Lord vpon the mount Oreb, that I should goe vnto Israel; but when I came vnto them, they set me at nought, and despised the commandement of the Lord.
text.NRSV: I, Ezra, received a command from the Lord on Mount Horeb to go to Israel. When I came to them they rejected me and refused the Lord’s commandment.
text.DRB: I Esdras received commandment of our Lord in mount Oreb, that I should go to Israel: to whom when I came, they refused me, & rejected the commandment of our Lord.
And therefore, I say unto you gentiles, which hear, and understand, Look for your pastor, he will give you the rest of eternity: because he is at hand, that shall come in the end of the world.
text.KJV: 2:34 And therefore I say vnto you, O yee heathen, that heare and vnderstand, Looke for your shepheard, hee shall giue you euerlasting rest; for he is nigh at hand, that shall come in the end of the world.
text.NRSV: Therefore I say to you, O nations that hear and understand, ‘Wait for your shepherd; he will give you everlasting rest, because he who will come at the end of the age is close at hand.

text.DRB: Be ye ready for the rewards of the Kingdom, because perpetual light shall shine to you for time everlasting.
text.KJV: 2:35 Be ready to the reward of the kingdome, for the euerlasting light shal shine vpon you for euermore.
text.NRSV: Be ready for the rewards of the kingdom, because perpetual light will shine on you for evermore.

text.DRB: Fly from the shadow of this world: receive ye the pleasantness of your glory. I openly call to witness my saviour.
text.KJV: 2:36 Flee the shadow of this world, receiue the ioyfulnesse of your glory: I testifie my Sauiour openly.
text.NRSV: Flee from the shadow of this age, receive the joy of your glory; I publicly call on my saviour to witness.]

text.DRB: Receive the commended gifts and be pleasant, giving thanks to him that called you to the heavenly Kingdoms.
text.KJV: 2:37 O receiue the gift that is giuen you, and be glad, giuing thankes vnto him that hath called you to the heauenly kingdome.
text.NRSV: Receive what the Lord has entrusted to you and be joyful, giving thanks to him who has called you to the celestial kingdoms.

text.DRB: Arise, & stand & see the number of them that are signed in the feast of our Lord.
text.KJV: 2:38 Arise vp and stand, behold the number of those that be sealed in the feast of the Lord:
text.NRSV: Rise, stand erect, and see the number of those who have been sealed at the feast of the Lord.

text.DRB: They that have transferred themselves from the shadow of the world, have received glorious garments of our Lord.
text.KJV: 2:39 Which are departed from the shadow of the world, and haue receiued glorious garments of the Lord.
text.NRSV: Those who have departed from the shadow of this age have received glorious garments from the Lord.

text.DRB: Receive o Sion thy number, and shut up thine made white, which have accomplished the law of our Lord.
text.KJV: 2:40 Take thy number, O Sion, and shut vp those of thine that are clothed in white, which haue fulfilled the Law of the Lord.
text.NRSV: Take again your full number, O Zion, and close the list of your people who are clothed in white, who have fulfilled the law of the Lord.

text.DRB: The number of thy children, which thou didst wish is full. Desire the power of our Lord that thy people may be sanctified, which was called from the beginning.
text.KJV: 2:41 The number of thy children whom thou longedst for, is fulfilled: beseech the power of the Lord, that thy people which haue been called from the beginning, may be hallowed.
text.NRSV: The number of your children, whom you desired, is now complete; implore the Lord’s authority that your people, who have been called from the beginning, may be made holy.’

text.KJV: 2:42 I Esdras saw vpon the mount Sion a great people, whom I could not number, and they all praised the Lord with songs.
text.NRSV: I, Ezra, saw on Mount Zion a great multitude that I could not number, and they all were praising the Lord with songs.
text.DRB: I Esdras saw in mount Sion a great multitude, which I could not number, and they did all praise our Lord with songs. Apoc. 7. v. 9.

text.KJV: 2:43 And in the middest of them there was a young man of a high stature, taller then all the rest, and vpon euery one of their heads he set crownes, and was more exalted, which I marueiled at greatly.
text.NRSV: In their midst was a young man of great stature, taller than any of the others, and on the head of each of them he placed a crown, but he was more exalted than they.
text.DRB: And in the midst of them was a young man high of stature, appearing above over them all, and he put crowns upon every one of their heads, and he was more exalted. And I was astonished at the miracle.
Then asked I an Angel, and said: Who are these
text.KJV: 2:44 So I asked the Angel, and said, Sir, what are these?
text.NRSV: Then I asked an angel, ‘Who are these, my lord?’

text.DRB: Who answering said to me: These are they that have laid off the mortal garment, and taken an immortal, and have confessed the name of God. Now they are crowned, and receive palms.
text.KJV: 2:45 Hee answered, and said vnto me, These be they that haue put off the mortall clothing, and put on the immortall, and haue confessed the Name of God: now are they crowned, and receiue palmes.
text.NRSV: He answered and said to me, ‘These are they who have put off mortal clothing and have put on the immortal, and have confessed the name of God. Now they are being crowned, and receive palms.’

text.DRB: And I said to the Angel: That young man what is he, which putteth the crowns upon them, & giveth palms into their hands?
text.KJV: 2:46 Then sayd I vnto the Angel, What yong person is it that crowneth them, and giueth them palmes in their handes?
text.NRSV: Then I said to the angel, ‘Who is that young man who is placing crowns on them and putting palms in their hands?’

text.KJV: 2:47 So hee answered, and said vnto me, It is the sonne of God, whom they haue confessed in the world. Then began I greatly to commend them, that stood so stiffely for the Name of the Lord.
text.NRSV: He answered and said to me, “He is the Son of God, whom they confessed in the world.” So I began to praise those who had stood valiantly for the name of the Lord.
text.DRB: And answering he said to me: The same is the Son of God, whom they did confess in the world: and I began to magnify them, that stood strongly for the name of our Lord.
Then said the Angel to me: Go, tell my people, what manner of marvelous things and how great thou hast seen of the Lord God.
text.KJV: 2:48 Then the Angel sayd vnto me, Goe thy way, and tell my people what maner of things, and how great wonders of the Lord thy God thou hast seene.
text.NRSV: Then the angel said to me, ‘Go, tell my people how great and how many are the wonders of the Lord God that you have seen.’

text.KJV: 3:1 In the thirtieth yeere after the ruine of the citie, I was in Babylon, and lay troubled vpon my bed, and my thoughts came vp ouer my heart.
text.NRSV: In the thirtieth year after the destruction of the city, I was in Babylon—I, Salathiel, who am also Ezra. I was troubled as I lay on my bed, and my thoughts welled up in my heart,

text.KJV: 3:2 For I saw the desolation of Sion, and the wealth of them that dwelt at Babylon.
text.NRSV: because I saw the desolation of Zion and the wealth of those who lived in Babylon.
text.DRB: because I saw the desolation of Sion, & the aboundance of them that dwelt in Babylon.
And my spirit was tossed exceedingly, & I began to speak to the highest timorous words,
text.KJV: 3:3 And my spirit was sore moued, so that I began to speake words full of feare to the most High, and said,
text.NRSV: My spirit was greatly agitated, and I began to speak anxious words to the Most High, and said,

text.DRB: and said: O Lord dominator that spakest from the beginning, when thou didst plant the earth, and that alone, & didst rule over the people, .
text.KJV: 3:4 O Lord, who bearest rule, thou spakest at the beginning, when thou didst plant the earth (and that thy selfe alone) and commandedst the people,
text.NRSV: ‘O sovereign Lord, did you not speak at the beginning when you planted] the earth—and that without help—and commanded the dust]

text.DRB: and gavest Adam a dead body, but that also was the work of thy hands, and didst breathe into him the spirit of life, and he was made to live before thee:
text.KJV: 3:5 And gauest a body vnto Adam without soule, which was the workemanship of thine hands, & didst breathe into him the breath of life, and he was made liuing before thee.
text.NRSV: and it gave you Adam, a lifeless body? Yet he was the creation of your hands, and you breathed into him the breath of life, and he was made alive in your presence.

text.DRB: and thou brought him into paradise, which thy right hand had planted, before the earth came.
text.KJV: 3:6 And thou leddest him into paradise, which thy right hand had planted, before euer the earth came forward.
text.NRSV: And you led him into the garden that your right hand had planted before the earth appeared.

text.DRB: And him thou didst command to love thy way, and he transgressed it, and forthwith thou didst institute death in him, and in his posterity, & there were born nations, and tribes, & peoples, and kindreds, whereof there is no number.
text.KJV: 3:7 And vnto him thou gauest commandement to loue thy way, which he transgressed, and immediatly thou appointedst death in him, and in his generations, of whom came nations, tribes, people, and kinreds out of number.
text.NRSV: And you laid upon him one commandment of yours; but he transgressed it, and immediately you appointed death for him and for his descendants. From him there sprang nations and tribes, peoples and clans without number.

text.DRB: And every nation walked in their own will, & they did marvelous things before thee, and despised thy precepts.
text.KJV: 3:8 And euery people walked after their owne will, and did wonderfull things before thee, and despised thy commandements.
text.NRSV: And every nation walked after its own will; they did ungodly things in your sight and rejected your commands, and you did not hinder them.

text.DRB: And again in time thou brought in the flood upon inhabitants of the world, and didst destroy them. .
text.KJV: 3:9 And againe in processe of time thou broughtest the flood vpon those that dwelt in the world, and destroyedst them.
text.NRSV: But again, in its time you brought the flood upon the inhabitants of the world and destroyed them.

text.DRB: And there was made in every one of them, as unto Adam to die, so to them the flood.
text.KJV: 3:10 And it came to passe in euery of them, that as death was to Adam, so was the flood to these.
text.NRSV: And the same fate befell all of them: just as death came upon Adam, so the flood upon them.

text.DRB: But thou didst leave one of them, Noe with his house, and to him were all the just.
text.KJV: 3:11 Neuerthelesse one of them thou leftest, namely Noah with his household, of whom came all righteous men.
text.NRSV: But you left one of them, Noah with his household, and all the righteous who have descended from him.

text.DRB: And it came to pass, when they began to be multiplied, that dwelt upon the earth, and multiplied children and peoples and many nations: and they began again to do impiety more than the former.
text.KJV: 3:12 And it happened, that when they that dwelt vpō the earth began to multiply, and had gotten them many children, and were a great people, they beganne againe to be more vngodly then the first.
text.NRSV: ‘When those who lived on earth began to multiply, they produced children and peoples and many nations, and again they began to be more ungodly than were their ancestors.

text.DRB: And it came to pass when they did iniquity before thee, thou didst choose thee a man of them, whose name was Abraham.
text.KJV: 3:13 Now when they liued so wickedly before thee, thou diddest choose thee a man from among them, whose name was Abraham.
text.NRSV: And when they were committing iniquity in your sight, you chose for yourself one of them, whose name was Abraham;

text.DRB: And thou didst love him and to him only thou didst shew thy will. .
text.KJV: 3:14 Him thou louedst, and vnto him onely thou shewedst thy will:
text.NRSV: you loved him, and to him alone you revealed the end of the times, secretly by night.

text.DRB: And thou didst dispose unto him an everlasting testament, and toldst him that thou wouldst never forsake his seed. And thou gavest him Isaac, and to Isaac thou gavest Jacob and Esau.
text.KJV: 3:15 And madest an euerlasting couenant with him, promising him that thou wouldest neuer forsake his seede.
text.NRSV: You made an everlasting covenant with him, and promised him that you would never forsake his descendants; and you gave him Isaac, and to Isaac you gave Jacob and Esau.

text.DRB: And Jacob thou didst sever to thyself, but Esau thou didst separate. And Jacob grew to a great multitude.
text.KJV: 3:16 And vnto him, thou gauest Isahac, and vnto Isahac also thou gauest Iacob and Esau. As for Iacob thou didst choose him to thee, and put by Esau: and so Iacob became a great multitude.
text.NRSV: You set apart Jacob for yourself, but Esau you rejected; and Jacob became a great multitude.

text.DRB: And it came to pass when thou didst bring forth his seed out of Ægypt, thou brought it upon mount Sinai. .
text.KJV: 3:17 And it came to passe, that when thou leddest his seede out of Egypt, thou broughtest them vp to the mount Sina.
text.NRSV: And when you led his descendants out of Egypt, you brought them to Mount Sinai.

text.DRB: And thou didst bow the heavens, and fasten the earth, and didst shake the world, and madest the depths to tremble, and troubledst the world,
text.KJV: 3:18 And bowing the heauens, thou didest set fast the earth, mouedst the whole world, and madest the depth to tremble, and troubledst the men of that age.
text.NRSV: You bent down the heavens and shook] the earth, and moved the world, and caused the depths to tremble, and troubled the times.

text.DRB: and thy glory passed four gates of fire, and of earthquake, and wind, and frost, that thou mightst give a law to the seed of Jacob, and to the generation of Israel diligence.
text.KJV: 3:19 And thy glory went through foure gates, of fire, and of earthquake, and of wind, and of cold, that thou mightest giue the law vnto the seed of Iacob, and diligence vnto the generation of Israel.
text.NRSV: Your glory passed through the four gates of fire and earthquake and wind and ice, to give the law to the descendants of Jacob, and your commandment to the posterity of Israel.

text.KJV: 3:20 And yet tookest thou not away from them a wicked heart, that thy law might bring forth fruite in them.
text.NRSV: “Yet you did not take away their evil heart from them, so that your law might bring forth fruit in them.”
text.DRB: And thou didst not take away from them a malignant heart, that thy law might bring forth fruit in them.
For Adam the first bearing a vicious heart transgressed and was overcome, yea and all that were born of him.
text.KJV: 3:21 For the first Adam bearing a wicked heart transgressed, and was ouercome; and so be all they that are borne of him.
text.NRSV: For the first Adam, burdened with an evil heart, transgressed and was overcome, as were also all who were descended from him.

text.DRB: And it was made a permanent
text.KJV: 3:22 Thus infirmity was made permanent; and the law (also) in the heart of the people with the malignity of the roote, so that the good departed away, and the euill abode still.
text.NRSV: Thus the disease became permanent; the law was in the hearts of the people along with the evil root; but what was good departed, and the evil remained.

text.DRB: And the times passed, and the years were ended: and thou didst raise up unto thee a servant named David,
text.KJV: 3:23 So the times passed away, and the yeeres were brought to an end: then diddest thou raise thee vp a seruant, called Dauid,
text.NRSV: So the times passed and the years were completed, and you raised up for yourself a servant, named David.

text.DRB: and spakest unto him to build a city of thy name, and to offer unto thee in it frankincense, and oblations.
text.KJV: 3:24 Whom thou commandedst to build a citie vnto thy name, and to offer incense and oblations vnto thee therein.
text.NRSV: You commanded him to build a city for your name, and there to offer you oblations from what is yours.

text.DRB: And this was done many years, and they that inhabited the city forsook thee,
text.KJV: 3:25 When this was done many yeeres, then they that inhabited the citie forsooke thee,
text.NRSV: This was done for many years; but the inhabitants of the city transgressed,

text.DRB: in all things as Adam and all his generations. For they also used a wicked heart.
text.KJV: 3:26 And in all things did euen as Adam, and all his generations had done, for they also had a wicked heart.
text.NRSV: in everything doing just as Adam and all his descendants had done, for they also had the evil heart.

text.DRB: And thou didst deliver thy city into the hands of thine enemies.
text.KJV: 3:27 And so thou gauest the citie ouer into the hands of thine enemies.
text.NRSV: So you handed over your city to your enemies.

text.DRB: Why, do they better things, that inhabit Babylon? And for this shall she rule over Sion? .
text.KJV: 3:28 Are their deeds then any better that inhabite Babylon, that they should therefore haue the dominion ouer Sion?
text.NRSV: ‘Then I said in my heart, Are the deeds of those who inhabit Babylon any better? Is that why it has gained dominion over Zion?

text.DRB: It came to pass when I was come hither, & had seen the impieties that cannot be numbered: and my soul saw many offending this thirtieth year, and my heart was astonished:
text.KJV: 3:29 For when I came thither, and had seene impieties without number, then my soule saw many euill doers in this thirtieth yeere, so that my heart failed me.
text.NRSV: For when I came here I saw ungodly deeds without number, and my soul has seen many sinners during these thirty years.] And my heart failed me,

text.DRB: because I saw how thou bearest with their sin, and didst spare them that did impiously, and didst destroy thine own people, and preserve thine enemies, and didst not signify it.
text.KJV: 3:30 For I haue seene how thou sufferest them sinning, and hast spared wicked doers: and hast destroyed thy people, and hast preserued thine enemies, and hast not signified it.
text.NRSV: because I have seen how you endure those who sin, and have spared those who act wickedly, and have destroyed your people, and protected your enemies,

text.DRB: I nothing remember how this way should be forsaken: doth Babylon better things than Sion?
text.KJV: 3:31 I doe not remember how this way may be left: Are they then of Babylon better then they of Sion?
text.NRSV: and have not shown to anyone how your way may be comprehended.] Are the deeds of Babylon better than those of Zion?

text.DRB: Or hath any nation known thee beside Israel: or what tribes have believed thy testaments as Jacob?
text.KJV: 3:32 Or is there any other people that knoweth thee besides Israel? or what generation hath so beleeued thy Couenants as Iacob?
text.NRSV: Or has another nation known you besides Israel? Or what tribes have so believed the covenants as these tribes of Jacob?

text.DRB: Whose reward hath not appeared, nor their labour fructified. For passing through I passed among the nations, & I saw them abound, & not mindful of thy commandments.
text.KJV: 3:33 And yet their reward appeareth not, and their labour hath no fruite: for I haue gone here and there through the heathen, and I see that they flowe in wealth, and think not vpon thy commandements.
text.NRSV: Yet their reward has not appeared and their labour has borne no fruit. For I have travelled widely among the nations and have seen that they abound in wealth, though they are unmindful of your commandments.

text.DRB: Now therefore weigh our iniquities in a balance, and theirs that dwell in the world: and thy name shall not be found, but in Israel.
text.KJV: 3:34 Weigh thou therfore our wickednesse now in the ballance, and theirs also that dwell in the world: and so shall thy Name no where be found, but in Israel.
text.NRSV: Now therefore, weigh in a balance our iniquities and those of the inhabitants of the world; and it will be found which way the turn of the scale will incline.

text.DRB: Or when have not they sinned in thy sight, that inhabit the earth? Or what nation hath so observed thy commandments?
text.KJV: 3:35 Or when was it that they which dwell vpon the earth, haue not sinned in thy sight? or what people hath so kept thy commandements?
text.NRSV: When have the inhabitants of the earth not sinned in your sight? Or what nation has kept your commandments so well?

text.KJV: 3:36 Thou shalt find that Israel by name hath kept thy precepts: but not the heathen.
text.NRSV: “You may indeed find individual mortals who have kept your commandments, but nations you will not find.”
text.DRB: These certes by their names thou shalt find to have kept thy commandments, but the nations thou shalt not find.

text.KJV: 4:1 And the Angel that was sent vnto me, whose name was Uriel, gaue mee an answere,
text.NRSV: Then the angel that had been sent to me, whose name was Uriel, gave me an answer

text.KJV: 4:2 And said, Thy heart hath gone too farre in this world, and thinkest thou to comprehend the way of the most High?
text.NRSV: and said, “Your understanding has utterly failed regarding this world, and do you think you can comprehend the way of the Most High?”
text.DRB: and said to me: Thy heart exceeding hath exceeded in this world, and thou thinkest to comprehend the way of the Highest.
And I said: It is so my Lord. And he answered me, and said: I am sent to shew thee three ways, & to propose to thee three similitudes.
text.KJV: 4:3 Then said I, Yea my Lord: and he answered me and said, I am sent to shew thee three wayes, and to set forth three similitudes before thee.
text.NRSV: Then I said, ‘Yes, my lord.’ And he replied to me, ‘I have been sent to show you three ways, and to put before you three problems.

text.DRB: Of the which if thou shalt declare to me one of them, I also will shew thee the way which thou desirest to see, & will teach thee whence a wicked heart is.
text.KJV: 4:4 Whereof if thou canst declare me one, I will shew thee also the way that thou desirest to see, & I shall shew thee from whence the wicked heart cōmeth.
text.NRSV: If you can solve one of them for me, then I will show you the way you desire to see, and will teach you why the heart is evil.’

text.DRB: And I said, Speak my Lord. And he said to me: Go, weigh me the weight of the fire, or measure me the blast of the wind, or call me back the day that is past.
text.KJV: 4:5 And I said, Tel on my Lord. Then said he vnto me, Goe thy way, weigh me the weight of the fire, or measure me the blast of the wind, or call me againe the day that is past.
text.NRSV: And he said to me, ‘Go, weigh for me the weight of fire, or measure for me a blast] of wind, or call back for me the day that is past.’

text.DRB: And I answered, and said: what man born can do it, that thou askest me of these things?
text.KJV: 4:6 Then answered I and said, What man is able to doe that, that thou shouldest aske such things of mee?
text.NRSV: I answered and said, ‘Who of those that have been born can do that, that you should ask me about such things?’

text.DRB: And he said to me: If I should ask thee, saying: How great habitations are there in the heart of the sea, or how great veins be there in the beginning of the depth, or how great veins be there above the firmament, and what are the issues of paradise:
text.KJV: 4:7 And he said vnto me, If I should aske thee how great dwellings are in the midst of þ sea, or how many springs are in the beginning of the deepe, or how many springs are aboue the firmament, or which are the outgoings of Paradise:
text.NRSV: And he said to me, ‘If I had asked you, “How many dwellings are in the heart of the sea, or how many streams are at the source of the deep, or how many streams are above the firmament, or which are the exits of Hades, or which are the entrances] of paradise?”

text.DRB: thou wouldest perhaps say to me: I have not descended into the depth, nor into hell as yet, neither, have I ascended at any time into heaven.
text.KJV: 4:8 Peraduenture thou wouldest say vnto me, I neuer went downe into the deepe, nor as yet into hell, neither did I euer climbe vp into heauen.
text.NRSV: perhaps you would have said to me, “I never went down into the deep, nor as yet into Hades, neither did I ever ascend into heaven.”

text.DRB: But now I have not asked thee, saving of the fire, & the wind, and the day by the which thou hast passed, and from the which thou canst not be separated: and thou hast not answered me of them.
text.KJV: 4:9 Neuerthelesse, now haue I asked thee but onely of the fire and winde, and of the day where through thou hast passed, and of things frō which thou canst not be separated, and yet canst thou giue me uo answeere of them.
text.NRSV: But now I have asked you only about fire and wind and the day—things that you have experienced and from which you cannot be separated, and you have given me no answer about them.’

text.KJV: 4:10 He said moreouer vnto me, Thine owne things, and such as are growen vp with thee, canst thou not know.
text.NRSV: He said to me, “You cannot understand the things with which you have grown up;”
text.DRB: And he said to me: Thou canst not know the things that are thine which grow together with thee:

text.KJV: 4:11 How should thy vessel then bee able to comprehend the way of the highest, and the world being now outwardly corrupted, to vnderstand the corruption that is euident in my sight?
text.NRSV: “how then can your mind comprehend the way of the Most High? And how can one who is already worn out by the corrupt world understand incorruption?”
text.DRB: and how can thy vessel comprehend the way of the Highest, and now the world being outwardly corrupted, understand the corruption evident in my sight:
I said to him: Better were it for us not to be, than yet living to live in impieties, and to suffer, and not to understand for what thing.
text.KJV: 4:12 Then said I vnto him, It were better that we were not at all, then that we should liue still in wickednesse, and to suffer, and not to know wherefore.
text.NRSV: and said to him, ‘It would have been better for us not to be here than to come here and live in ungodliness, and to suffer and not understand why.’

text.DRB: And he answered me, and said: Going forth I went forward to a wood of trees in the field, and they devised a device, . 2. Par. 25.
text.KJV: 4:13 He answered me and said, I went into a forest into a plaine, and the trees tooke counsell,
text.NRSV: He answered me and said, ‘I went into a forest of trees of the plain, and they made a plan

text.DRB: And in like manner the waves of the sea they also devised a device, and said: Come let us go up, let us overthrow the woods of the field, that there also we may consummate another country for ourselves.
text.KJV: 4:15 The floods of the Sea also in like maner tooke counsell, and said, Come, let vs goe vp and subdue the woods of the plaine, that there also we may make vs another countrey.
text.NRSV: In like manner the waves of the sea also made a plan and said, “Come, let us go up and subdue the forest of the plain so that there also we may gain more territory for ourselves.”

text.DRB: And the wood's device was made vain, for fire came, and consumed it.
text.KJV: 4:16 The thought of the wood was in vaine, for the fire came and consumed it.
text.NRSV: But the plan of the forest was in vain, for the fire came and consumed it;

text.DRB: Likewise also the device of the waves of the sea. For the sand stood, & stayed them.
text.KJV: 4:17 The thought of the floods of the Sea came likewise to nought, for the sand stood vp and stopped them.
text.NRSV: likewise also the plan of the waves of the sea was in vain,] for the sand stood firm and blocked it.

text.DRB: For if thou wert judge of these, whom wouldest thou begin to justify, or whom to condemn?
text.KJV: 4:18 If thou wert iudge now betwixt these two, whom wouldest thou begin to iustifie, or whom wouldest thou condemne?
text.NRSV: If now you were a judge between them, which would you undertake to justify, and which to condemn?’

text.DRB: And I answered, and said: Verily, they devised a vain device. For the earth is given to the wood, and a place to the sea to carry her waves.
text.KJV: 4:19 I answered and said, Uerily it is a foolish thought that they both haue deuised: for the ground is giuen vnto the wood, and the sea also hath his place to beare his floods.
text.NRSV: I answered and said, ‘Each made a foolish plan, for the land has been assigned to the forest, and the locale of the sea a place to carry its waves.’

text.DRB: And he answered me, and said: Thou hast judged well, and why hast thou not judged for thyself?
text.KJV: 4:20 Then answered he me and said, Thou hast giuen a right iudgment, but why iudgest thou not thy selfe also?
text.NRSV: He answered me and said, ‘You have judged rightly, but why have you not judged so in your own case?

text.DRB: For as the earth is given to the wood, and the sea for the waves thereof: so they that inhabit upon the earth, can understand only the things that are upon the earth: and they upon the heavens, the things that are above the height of the heavens.
text.KJV: 4:21 For like as the ground is giuen vnto the wood, & the sea to his floods: euen so they that dwell vpon the earth may vnderstand nothing, but that which is vpon the earth: and hee that dwelleth aboue the heauens, may onely vnderstand the things that are aboue the height of the heauens.
text.NRSV: For as the land has been assigned to the forest and the sea to its waves, so also those who inhabit the earth can understand only what is on the earth, and he who is] above the heavens can understand what is above the height of the heavens.’

text.DRB: And I answered, and said: I beseech thee Lord, that sense may be given me to understand.
text.KJV: 4:22 Then answered I, and said, I beseech thee, O Lord, let me haue vnderstanding.
text.NRSV: Then I answered and said, ‘I implore you, my lord, why] have I been endowed with the power of understanding?

text.DRB: For I meant not to ask of thy superior things, but of those that pass by us daily. For what cause Israel is given into reproach to the gentiles, the people whom thou hast loved, is given to impious tribes, and the law of our fathers is brought to destruction, and the written ordinances are no where:
text.KJV: 4:23 For it was not my minde to be curious of the high things, but of such as passe by vs dayly, namely wherefore Israel is giuen vp as a reproch to the heathen, and for what cause the people whom thou hast loued, is giuen ouer vnto vngodly nations, and why the Lawe of our forefathers is brought to nought, and the written Couenants come to none effect.
text.NRSV: For I did not wish to inquire about the ways above, but about those things that we daily experience: why Israel has been given over to the Gentiles in disgrace; why the people whom you loved has been given over to godless tribes, and the law of our ancestors has been brought to destruction and the written covenants no longer exist.

text.DRB: And we have passed out of the world, as locusts, and our life is astonishment and dread, and we are not worthy to obtain mercy.
text.KJV: 4:24 And wee passe away out of the world as grassehoppers, and our life is astonishment and feare, and we are not worthy to obtaine mercie.
text.NRSV: We pass from the world like locusts, and our life is like a mist,] and we are not worthy to obtain mercy.

text.DRB: But what will he do to his name that is invocated upon us? And of these things I did ask.
text.KJV: 4:25 What will he then doe vnto his Name, whereby we are called? of these things haue I asked.
text.NRSV: But what will he do for his] name that is invoked over us? It is about these things that I have asked.’

text.KJV: 4:26 Then answered he me, and said, The more thou searchest, the more thou shalt marueile, for the world hasteth fast to passe away,
text.NRSV: He answered me and said, “If you are alive, you will see, and if you live long, you will often marvel; because the age is hurrying swiftly to its end.”
text.DRB: And he answered me, and said: If thou search very much, thou shalt often marvel: because the world hastening hasteneth to pass,
and cannot comprehend the things which in times to come are promised to the just: because this world is full of injustice and infirmities.
text.KJV: 4:27 And cannot comprehend the things that are promised to the righteous in time to come: for this world is ful of vnrighteousnesse and infirmities.
text.NRSV: It will not be able to bring the things that have been promised to the righteous in their appointed times, because this age is full of sadness and infirmities.

text.DRB: But concerning the things that thou demandest I will tell thee: for the evil is sowed, and the destruction thereof is not yet come.
text.KJV: 4:28 But as concerning the things whereof thou askest me, I wil tell thee; for the euil is sowen, but the destruction thereof is not yet come.
text.NRSV: For the evil about which] you ask me has been sown, but the harvest of it has not yet come.

text.DRB: If then that which is sown be not turned up, and the place depart where the evil is sown, that shall not come where the good is sown.
text.KJV: 4:29 If therefore that which is sowen, be not turned vpside downe; and if the place where the euil is sowen passe not away, then cannot it come that is sowen with good.
text.NRSV: If therefore that which has been sown is not reaped, and if the place where the evil has been sown does not pass away, the field where the good has been sown will not come.

text.DRB: Because the grain of ill seed hath been sown in the heart of Adam from the beginning: and how much impiety hath it engendered until now, and doth engender until the floor come?
text.KJV: 4:30 For the graine of euill seed hath bene sowen in the heart of Adam from the beginning, and how much vngodlinesse hath it brought vp vnto this time? and how much shall it yet bring foorth vntill the time of threshing come.
text.NRSV: For a grain of evil seed was sown in Adam’s heart from the beginning, and how much ungodliness it has produced until now—and will produce until the time of threshing comes!

text.DRB: And esteem with thyself the grain of the ill seed, how much fruit of impiety it hath engendered:
text.KJV: 4:31 Ponder now by thy selfe, how great fruit of wickednesse the graine of euil seed hath brought forth.
text.NRSV: Consider now for yourself how much fruit of ungodliness a grain of evil seed has produced.

text.DRB: When the ears shall be cut, which are innumerable, what a great floor will they begin to make?
text.KJV: 4:32 And when the eares shall bee cut downe, which are without number, how great a floore shall they fill?
text.NRSV: When heads of grain without number are sown, how great a threshing-floor they will fill!’

text.DRB: And I answered, and said: How, and when shall these things be? Why are our years few and evil?
text.KJV: 4:33 Then I answered and said, How and when shall these things come to passe? wherefore are our yeeres few and euill?
text.NRSV: Then I answered and said, ‘How long?] When will these things be? Why are our years few and evil?’

text.DRB: And he answered me, and said to me, Hasten not above the Highest. For thou doest hasten in vain to be above him, for thy excess is much.
text.KJV: 4:34 And he answered me, saying, Do not thou hasten aboue the most Highest: for thy haste is in vaine to be aboue him, for thou hast much exceeded.
text.NRSV: He answered me and said, ‘Do not be in a greater hurry than the Most High. You, indeed, are in a hurry for yourself,] but the Highest is in a hurry on behalf of many.

text.DRB: Did not the souls of the just in their cellars, ask of these things, saying: How hope I so, and when shall the fruit come of the floor of our reward?
text.KJV: 4:35 Did not the soules also of the righteous aske question of these things in their chambers, saying, How long shall I hope on this fashion? when commeth the fruit of the floore of our reward?
text.NRSV: Did not the souls of the righteous in their chambers ask about these matters, saying, “How long are we to remain here?] And when will the harvest of our reward come?”

text.DRB: And Jeremiel the Archangel answered to those things, and said: When the number of the seeds in you shall be filled, because he hath weighed the world in a balance,
text.KJV: 4:36 And vnto these things Uriel the Archangel gaue them answere, and said, Euen when the number of seedes is filled in you: for he hath weighed the world in the ballance.
text.NRSV: And the archangel Jeremiel answered and said, “When the number of those like yourselves is completed;] for he has weighed the age in the balance,

text.DRB: and with a measure hath he measured the times, and in number he hath numbered the times, & hath not moved, nor stirred them, until the foresaid measure be filled.
text.KJV: 4:37 By measure hath hee measured the times, and by number hath he numbred the times; and he doeth not mooue nor stirre them, vntill the said measure be fulfilled.
text.NRSV: and measured the times by measure, and numbered the times by number; and he will not move or arouse them until that measure is fulfilled.”’

text.DRB: And I answered, & said. O Lord Dominator, we also are all full of impiety.
text.KJV: 4:38 Then answered I, and said, O Lord that bearest rule, euen we all are full of impietie.
text.NRSV: Then I answered and said, ‘But, O sovereign Lord, all of us also are full of ungodliness.

text.DRB: And lest perhaps for us the floors of the just be not filled, for the sins of the inhabitants upon the earth.
text.KJV: 4:39 And for our sakes peraduenture it is that the floores of the righteous are not filled, because of the sinnes of them that dwell vpon the earth.
text.NRSV: It is perhaps on account of us that the time of threshing is delayed for the righteous—on account of the sins of those who inhabit the earth.’

text.DRB: And he answered me, and said: Go, and ask a woman with child, if when she hath accomplished her nine months, her womb can yet hold the infant within it?
text.KJV: 4:40 So he answered me, and said, Go thy way to a wman with childe, and aske of her, when she hath fulfilled her nine moneths, if her wombe may keepe the birth any longer within her?
text.NRSV: He answered me and said, ‘Go and ask a pregnant woman whether, when her nine months have been completed, her womb can keep the foetus within her any longer.’

text.DRB: And I said it cannot Lord. And he said to me: In hell the cellars of the souls are like to the matrice.
text.KJV: 4:41 Then said I, No Lord, that can she not. And he said vnto mee, In the graue, the chambers of soules are like the wombe of a woman:
text.NRSV: He said to me, ‘In Hades the chambers of the souls are like the womb.

text.DRB: For as she that is in travail maketh haste, to escape the necessity of travailing: so this also hasteneth to render those things which are commended to it.
text.KJV: 4:42 For like as a woman that trauaileth, maketh haste to escape the necessitie of the trauaile: euen so doe these places haste to deliuer those things that are committed vnto them.
text.NRSV: For just as a woman who is in labour makes haste to escape the pangs of birth, so also do these places hasten to give back those things that were committed to them from the beginning.

text.DRB: From the beginning it shall be shewed thee touching those things, which thou doest covet to see.
text.KJV: 4:43 From the beginning looke what thou desirest to see, it shalbe shewed thee.
text.NRSV: Then the things that you desire to see will be disclosed to you.’

text.DRB: And I answered and said: If I have found grace before thine eyes, and if it be possible, and if I be fit,
text.KJV: 4:44 Then answered I, and said, If I haue found fauour in thy sight, and if it be possible, and if I be meet therefore,
text.NRSV: I answered and said, ‘If I have found favour in your sight, and if it is possible, and if I am worthy,

text.DRB: shew me if there be more to come than is passed, or more things have passed, than are to come.
text.KJV: 4:45 Shew me then whether there be more to come then is past, or more past then is to come.
text.NRSV: show me this also: whether more time is to come than has passed, or whether for us the greater part has gone by.

text.DRB: What passed, I know: but what is to come, I know not.
text.KJV: 4:46 What is past I know; but what is for to come I know not.
text.NRSV: For I know what has gone by, but I do not know what is to come.’

text.DRB: And he said to me: Stand upon the right side, and I will shew thee the interpretation of the similitude.
text.KJV: 4:47 And he said vnto me, Stand vp vpon the right side, and I shal expound the similitude vnto you.
text.NRSV: And he said to me, ‘Stand at my right side, and I will show you the interpretation of a parable.’

text.DRB: And I stood, and saw: and behold a burning furnace passed before me, and it came to pass
text.KJV: 4:48 So I stood and saw, and behold an hot burning ouen passed by before mee: and it happened that when the flame was gone by, I looked, and behold, the smoke remained still.
text.NRSV: So I stood and looked, and lo, a flaming furnace passed by before me, and when the flame had gone by I looked, and lo, the smoke remained.

text.DRB: After these things there passed before me a cloud full of water, and with violence casting in much rain: and when the violence of rain wast cast, the drops therein overcame.
text.KJV: 4:49 After this there passed by before me a watrie cloude, and sent downe much raine with a storme, and when the stormie raine was past, the drops remained still.
text.NRSV: And after this a cloud full of water passed before me and poured down a heavy and violent rain, and when the violent rainstorm had passed, drops still remained in the cloud.]

text.DRB: And he said to me: Think with thyself, as the rain increaseth more than the drops, and the fire than the smoke: so did the measure that passed, more abound. But the drops, and the smoke overcame:
text.KJV: 4:50 Then said he vnto me, Consider with thy selfe: as the raine is more then the drops, and as the fire is greater then the smoke: but the drops and the smoke remaine behind: so the quantity which is past, did more exceede.
text.NRSV: He said to me, ‘Consider it for yourself; for just as the rain is more than the drops, and the fire is greater than the smoke, so the quantity that passed was far greater; but drops and smoke remained.’

text.DRB: and I prayed, and said, shall I live thinkest thou until these days? Or what shall be in those days?
text.KJV: 4:51 Then I prayed, and sayd, May I liue, thinkest thou, vntill that time? or what shall happen in those dayes?
text.NRSV: Then I prayed and said, ‘Do you think that I shall live until those days? Or who will be alive in those days?’

text.DRB: He answered me, and said: Of the signs whereof thou askest me, in part I can tell thee, howbeit of thy life I was not sent to tell thee, neither do I know.
text.KJV: 4:52 He answered me, and sayd, As for the tokens whereof thou askest me, I may tell thee of them in part; but as touching thy life, I am not sent to shew thee, for I doe not know it.
text.NRSV: He answered me and said, ‘Concerning the signs about which you ask me, I can tell you in part; but I was not sent to tell you concerning your life, for I do not know.

text.KJV: 5:1 Neuertheles as concerning the tokens, beholde, the dayes shall come that they which dwell vpon earth, shall bee taken in a great number, and the way of trueth shall be hidden, and the land shall be barren of faith.
text.NRSV: “Now concerning the signs: behold, the days are coming when those who inhabit the earth shall be seized with great terror, and the way of truth shall be hidden, and the land shall be barren of faith.”

text.DRB: And injustice shall be multiplied above that which thyself seest, and above that which thou hast heard in time past. Math. 24.
text.KJV: 5:2 But iniquitie shalbe increased aboue that which now thou seest, or that thou hast heard long agoe.
text.NRSV: Unrighteousness shall be increased beyond what you yourself see, and beyond what you heard of formerly.

text.DRB: And they shall put their foot into the country which now thou seest to reign, and they shall see it desolate.
text.KJV: 5:3 And the land that thou seest now to haue roote, shalt thou see wasted suddenly.
text.NRSV: And the land that you now see ruling shall be a trackless waste, and people shall see it desolate.

text.KJV: 5:4 But if the most high graunt thee to liue, thou shalt see after the third trumpet, that the Sunne shall suddenly shine againe in the night, and the Moone thrice in the day.
text.NRSV: “But if the Most High grants that you live, you shall see it thrown into confusion after the third period; and the sun shall suddenly begin to shine at night, and the moon during the day.”
text.DRB: And if the Highest give thee life, thou shalt see after the third trumpet, and the sun shall suddenly shine again in the night, and the moon thrice in a day,
and out of wood blood shall distill, and the stone shall give his voice, and the peoples shall be moved:
text.KJV: 5:5 And blood shal drop out of wood, and the stone shall giue his voice, and the people shalbe troubled.
text.NRSV: and the stars shall fall.]

text.DRB: and he reign, whom they hope not that inhabit upon the earth, and fowls shall make their flight away.
text.KJV: 5:6 And enen he shal rule whom they looke not for that dwel vpon the earth, and the foules shall take their flight away together.
text.NRSV: And one shall reign whom those who inhabit the earth do not expect, and the birds shall fly away together;

text.DRB: and the sea of Sodom shall cast the fishes, and shall make a noise in the night, which many knew not, and all shall hear the voice thereof,
text.KJV: 5:7 And the Sodomitish sea shall cast out fish, and make a noyse in the night, which many haue not knowen: but they shall all heare the voice thereof.
text.NRSV: and the Dead Sea] shall cast up fish; and one whom the many do not know shall make his voice heard by night, and all shall hear his voice.]

text.DRB: and there shall be made a confusion in many places, and the fire shall often be sent back, and the savage beasts shall go to other places, and women in their monthly flowers shall bring forth monsters,
text.KJV: 5:8 There shall be a confusion also in many places, and the fire shalbe oft sent out againe, and the wilde beasts shall change their places, and menstruous women shall bring foorth monsters.
text.NRSV: There shall be chaos also in many places, fire shall often break out, the wild animals shall roam beyond their haunts, and menstruous women shall bring forth monsters.

text.DRB: and in sweet waters shall salt waters be found, and all friends shall overthrow one another: and then shall wit be hid, and understanding shall be separated into his cellar:
text.KJV: 5:9 And salt waters shall be found in the sweete, and all friends shall destroy one another: then shall wit hide it selfe, and vnderstanding withdraw it selfe into his secret chamber,
text.NRSV: Salt waters shall be found in the sweet, and all friends shall conquer one another; then shall reason hide itself, and wisdom shall withdraw into its chamber,

text.DRB: and it shall be sought of many, & shall not be found: and injustice shall be multiplied, and incontinency upon the earth.
text.KJV: 5:10 And shall be sought of many, and yet not be found: then shall vnrighteousnesse and incontinencie be multiplyed vpon earth.
text.NRSV: and it shall be sought by many but shall not be found, and unrighteousness and unrestraint shall increase on earth.

text.DRB: And one country shall ask her neighbour, and shall say: Hath justice doing just passed through thee? And she shall deny it.
text.KJV: 5:11 One land also shall aske another, and say, Is righteousnes that maketh a man righteous, gone through thee? And it shall say, No.
text.NRSV: One country shall ask its neighbour, “Has righteousness, or anyone who does right, passed through you?” And it will answer, “No.”

text.DRB: And it shall be in that time, men shall hope, & shall not obtain: they shall labour, & their ways shall not have success.
text.KJV: 5:12 At the same time shall men hope, but nothing obtaine: they shall labour, but their wayes shall not prosper.
text.NRSV: At that time people shall hope but not obtain; they shall labour, but their ways shall not prosper.

text.DRB: These signs I am permitted to tell thee: and if thou pray again and weep, as also now, and fast seven days, thou shalt hear again greater things than these.
text.KJV: 5:13 To shew thee such tokens I haue leaue: and if thou wilt pray againe, and weepe as now, and fast seuen dayes, thou shalt heare yet greater things.
text.NRSV: These are the signs that I am permitted to tell you, and if you pray again, and weep as you do now, and fast for seven days, you shall hear yet greater things than these.’

text.DRB: And I awaked, and my body did shiver exceedingly: and my soul laboured, that it fainted:
text.KJV: 5:14 Then I awaked, & an extreme fearefulnesse went through all my body, and my minde was troubled, so that it fainted.
text.NRSV: Then I woke up, and my body shuddered violently, and my soul was so troubled that it fainted.

text.DRB: and the Angel that came, that spake in me, held me, and strengthened me, and set me upon my feet.
text.KJV: 5:15 So the Angel that was come to talke with me, helde me, comforted me, and set me vp vpon my feete.
text.NRSV: But the angel who had come and talked with me held me and strengthened me and set me on my feet.

text.DRB: And it came to pass in the second night, and Salathiel the Prince of the people came to me, and said to me: Where wast thou? And why is thy countenance heavy?
text.KJV: 5:16 And in the second night it came to passe, that Salathiel the captaine of the people came vnto mee, saying, Where hast thou beene? and why is thy countenance so heauie?
text.NRSV: Now on the second night Phaltiel, a chief of the people, came to me and said, ‘Where have you been? And why is your face sad?

text.DRB: Knowest thou not that Israel is committed to the in the country of their transmigration?
text.KJV: 5:17 Knowest thou not that Israel is committed vnto thee, in the land of their captiuitie?
text.NRSV: Or do you not know that Israel has been entrusted to you in the land of their exile?
