text.KJV: 8:28 Thinke not vpon those that haue walked fainedly before thee: but remember them, which according to thy will haue knowen thy feare.
text.NRSV: Do not think of those who have lived wickedly in your sight, but remember those who have willingly acknowledged that you are to be feared.

text.DRB: Neither be thou willing to destroy them that have had the manners of beasts: but respect them that have taught thy law gloriously.
text.KJV: 8:29 Let it not bee thy will to destroy them, which haue liued like beasts: but to looke vpon them that haue clearely taught thy Law.
text.NRSV: Do not will the destruction of those who have the ways of cattle, but regard those who have gloriously taught your law.]

text.DRB: Neither have indignation towards them, which are judged worse than beasts: but love them that always have confidence in thy justice and glory.
text.KJV: 8:30 Take thou no indignation at them which are deemed worse then beasts: but loue them that alway put their trust in thy righteousnesse, and glory.
text.NRSV: Do not be angry with those who are deemed worse than wild animals, but love those who have always put their trust in your glory.

text.DRB: Because we and our fathers languish with such diseases: but thou for sinners shalt be called merciful.
text.KJV: 8:31 For we and our fathers doe languish of such diseases; but because of vs sinners, thou shalt be called mercifull.
text.NRSV: For we and our ancestors have passed our lives in ways that bring death;] but it is because of us sinners that you are called merciful.

text.DRB: For if thou shalt be desirous to have mercy on us, then thou shalt be called merciful, to us having no works of justice.
text.KJV: 8:32 For if thou hast a desire to haue mercy vpon vs, thou shalt bee called mercifull, to vs namely, that haue no workes of righteousnesse.
text.NRSV: For if you have desired to have pity on us, who have no works of righteousness, then you will be called merciful.

text.DRB: For the just which
text.KJV: 8:33 For the iust which haue many good workes layed vp with thee, shall out of their owne deedes receiue reward.
text.NRSV: For the righteous, who have many works laid up with you, shall receive their reward in consequence of their own deeds.

text.DRB: For what is man, that thou art angry with him: or the corruptible kind, that thou art so bitter touching it?
text.KJV: 8:34 For what is man that thou shouldest take displeasure at him? or what is a corruptible generation, that thou shouldest be so bitter toward it?
text.NRSV: But what are mortals, that you are angry with them; or what is a corruptible race, that you are so bitter against it?

text.DRB: For in truth there is no man of them that be born, which hath not done impiously, and of them that confess, which have not sinned. 2.
text.KJV: 8:35 For in trueth there is no man among them that be borne, but he hath dealt wickedly, and among the faithfull, there is none which hath not done amisse.
text.NRSV: For in truth there is no one among those who have been born who has not acted wickedly; among those who have existed] there is no one who has not done wrong.

text.DRB: For in this shall thy justice be declared, and thy goodness, o Lord, when thou shalt have mercy on them, that have no substance of good works.
text.KJV: 8:36 For in this, O Lord, thy righteousnesse, and thy goodnesse shalbe declared, if thou be mercifull vnto them which haue not the confidence of good workes.
text.NRSV: For in this, O Lord, your righteousness and goodness will be declared, when you are merciful to those who have no store of good works.’

text.DRB: And he answered me, and said: Thou hast spoken some things rightly: and according to thy words, so also shall it be done,
text.KJV: 8:37 Then answered he mee, and said, Some things hast thou spoken aright, and according vnto thy words it shalbe.
text.NRSV: He answered me and said, ‘Some things you have spoken rightly, and it will turn out according to your words.

text.DRB: because I will not indeed think upon the work of them that have sinned before death, before the judgement, before perdition:
text.KJV: 8:38 For indeed I will not thinke on the disposition of them which haue sinned before death, before iudgement, before destruction.
text.NRSV: For indeed I will not concern myself about the fashioning of those who have sinned, or about their death, their judgement, or their destruction;

text.DRB: but I will rejoice upon the creature of the just, and I will remember their pilgrimage also, and salvation, and receiving of reward.
text.KJV: 8:39 But I will reioyce ouer the disposition of the righteous, and I wil remember also their pilgrimage, and the saluation, and the reward that they shall haue.
text.NRSV: but I will rejoice over the creation of the righteous, over their pilgrimage also, and their salvation, and their receiving their reward.

text.DRB: Therefore as I have spoken, so also it is.
text.KJV: 8:40 Like as I haue spoken now, so shall it come to passe.
text.NRSV: As I have spoken, therefore, so it shall be.

text.DRB: For as the husbandman soweth upon the ground many seeds, and planteth many plants, but not all which were sown in time are preserved, nor yet all that were planted shall take root: so they also that are sown in the world, shall not all be saved. . & 20.
text.KJV: 8:41 For as the husbandmau soweth much seed vpon the ground, and planteth many trees, and yet the thing that is sowen good in his season, commeth not vp, neither doeth all that is planted take root: euen so is it of them that are sowen in the world, they shall not all be saued.
text.NRSV: ‘For just as the farmer sows many seeds in the ground and plants a multitude of seedlings, and yet not all that have been sown will come up] in due season, and not all that were planted will take root; so also those who have been sown in the world will not all be saved.’

text.DRB: And I answered, and said: If I have found grace, let me speak.
text.KJV: 8:42 I answered then, and said, If I haue found grace, let me speake.
text.NRSV: I answered and said, ‘If I have found favour in your sight, let me speak.

text.DRB: As the seed of the husbandman, if it come not up, or receive not the rain in time, if it be corrupted with much rain, perisheth:
text.KJV: 8:43 Like as the husbandmans seede perisheth, if it come not vp, and receiue not the raine in due season, or if there come too much raine and corrupt it:
text.NRSV: If the farmer’s seed does not come up, because it has not received your rain in due season, or if it has been ruined by too much rain, it perishes.]

text.DRB: so likewise also man who made with thy hands, and thou named his image: because thou art likened to him, for whom thou hast made all things, and hast likened him to the seed of the husbandman.
text.KJV: 8:44 Euen so perisheth man also which is formed with thy hands, and is called thine owne image, because thou art like vnto him, for whose sake thou hast made all things, and likened him vnto the husbandmans seede.
text.NRSV: But people, who have been formed by your hands and are called your own image because they are made like you, and for whose sake you have formed all things—have you also made them like the farmer’s seed?

text.DRB: Be not angry upon us, but spare thy people, and have mercy on thy inheritance. And thou hast mercy on thy creature.
text.KJV: 8:45 Be not wroth with vs, but spare thy people, and haue mercy vpon thine owne inheritance: for thou art mercifull vnto thy creature.
text.NRSV: Surely not, O Lord] above! But spare your people and have mercy on your inheritance, for you have mercy on your own creation.’

text.DRB: And he answered me, and said: The things that are present to them that are present, and that shall be, to them that shall be.
text.KJV: 8:46 Then answered he me, and said, Things present are for the present, and things to come, for such as be to come.
text.NRSV: He answered me and said, ‘Things that are present are for those who live now, and things that are future are for those who will live hereafter.

text.DRB: For thou lackest much to be able to love my creature above me: and to thee often times, even to thyself I have approached, but to the unjust never.
text.KJV: 8:47 For thou commest farre short, that thou shouldest be able to loue my creature more then I: but I haue oft times drawen nigh vnto thee, and vnto it, but neuer to the vnrighteous.
text.NRSV: For you come far short of being able to love my creation more than I love it. But you have often compared yourself] to the unrighteous. Never do so!

text.DRB: But in this also thou art marvelous before the Highest,
text.KJV: 8:48 In this also thou art marueilous before the most high;
text.NRSV: But even in this respect you will be praiseworthy before the Most High,

text.DRB: because thou hast humbled thyself as becometh thee: and hast not judged thyself, that among the just thou mayst be very much glorified.
text.KJV: 8:49 In that thou hast humbled thy selfe as it becommeth thee, and hast not iudged thy selfe worthy to be much glorified among the righteous.
text.NRSV: because you have humbled yourself, as is becoming for you, and have not considered yourself to be among the righteous. You will receive the greatest glory,

text.DRB: For which cause many miseries, and miserable things shall be done to them that inhabit the world in the later days: because they have walked in much pride.
text.KJV: 8:50 For many great miseries shall be done to them, that in the latter time shal dwell in the world, because they haue walked in great pride.
text.NRSV: for many miseries will affect those who inhabit the world in the last times, because they have walked in great pride.

text.DRB: But thou for thyself understand, and for them that are like unto thee seek glory.
text.KJV: 8:51 But vnderstand thou for thy selfe, and seeke out the glory for such as be like thee.
text.NRSV: But think of your own case, and inquire concerning the glory of those who are like yourself,

text.DRB: For to you paradise is open, the tree of life is planted, time to come is prepared, aboundance is prepared, a city is builded, rest is approved, goodness is perfited, & perfit wisdom.
text.KJV: 8:52 For vnto you is Paradise opened, the tree of life is planted, the time to come is prepared, plenteousnesse is made ready, a citie is builded, and rest is allowed, yea perfect goodnesse and wisedome.
text.NRSV: because it is for you that paradise is opened, the tree of life is planted, the age to come is prepared, plenty is provided, a city is built, rest is appointed,] goodness is established, and wisdom perfected beforehand.

text.DRB: The root of evil is signed from you: infirmity, & moth is hid from you: and corruption is fled into hell in oblivion.
text.KJV: 8:53 The root of euil is sealed vp from you, weakenesse and the moth is hidde from you, and corruption is fled into hell to be forgotten.
text.NRSV: The root of evil] is sealed up from you, illness is banished from you, and death] is hidden; Hades has fled and corruption has been forgotten;]

text.DRB: Sorrows are past, & the treasure of immortality is shewed in the end.
text.KJV: 8:54 Sorrows are passed, & in the end is shewed the treasure of immortalitie.
text.NRSV: sorrows have passed away, and in the end the treasure of immortality is made manifest.

text.DRB: Add not therefore inquiring of the multitude of them that perish.
text.KJV: 8:55 And therefore aske thou no more questions concerning the multitude of them that perish.
text.NRSV: Therefore do not ask any more questions about the great number of those who perish.

text.DRB: For they also receiving liberty have despised the Highest, and contemned his law, and forsaken his ways.
text.KJV: 8:56 For when they had taken liberty, they despised the most High, thought scorne of his Lawe, and forsooke his wayes.
text.NRSV: For when they had opportunity to choose, they despised the Most High, and were contemptuous of his law, and abandoned his ways.

text.DRB: Yea and moreover they have trodden down his just ones.
text.KJV: 8:57 Moreouer, they haue troden downe his righteous,
text.NRSV: Moreover, they have even trampled on his righteous ones,

text.DRB: and have said in their heart, that there is no God: and that, knowing that they die. . & 52.
text.KJV: 8:58 And said in their heart, that there is no God, yea and that knowing they must die.
text.NRSV: and said in their hearts that there is no God—though they knew well that they must die.

text.DRB: For as the things aforesaid shall receive you: so thirst and torment, which are prepared shall take them: for he would not man to be destroyed.
text.KJV: 8:59 For as the things aforesaid shall receiue you, so thirst and paine are prepared for them; for it was not his will that men should come to nought.
text.NRSV: For just as the things that I have predicted await] you, so the thirst and torment that are prepared await them. For the Most High did not intend that anyone should be destroyed;

text.DRB: But they themselves also which are created, have defiled his name which made them: and have been unkind to him that prepared life.
text.KJV: 8:60 But they which be created, haue defiled the Name of him that made them, and were vnthankefull vnto him which prepared life for them.
text.NRSV: but those who were created have themselves defiled the name of him who made them, and have been ungrateful to him who prepared life for them now.

text.DRB: Wherefore my judgement now approacheth.
text.KJV: 8:61 And therefore is my iudgement now at hand.
text.NRSV: Therefore my judgement is now drawing near;

text.DRB: Which things I have not shewed to all, but to thee, and to few like unto thee. And I answered, and said:
text.KJV: 8:62 These things haue I not shewed vnto all men, but vnto thee, and a fewe like thee. Then answered I, and said,
text.NRSV: Then I answered and said,

text.DRB: Behold now Lord thou hast shewed me a multitude of signs, which thou wilt begin to do in the latter times, but thou hast not shewed me at what time.
text.KJV: 8:63 Behold, O Lord, now hast thou shewed me the multitude of the wonders which thou wilt begin to doe in the last times: but at what time, thou hast not shewed me.
text.NRSV: ‘O Lord, you have already shown me a great number of the signs that you will do in the last times, but you have not shown me when you will do them.’

text.KJV: 9:1 Hee answered me then, and sayde, Measure thou the time diligently in it selfe: and when thou seest part of the signes past, which I haue tolde thee before,
text.NRSV: He answered me and said, “Measure carefully in your mind, and when you see that some of the predicted signs have passed,”

text.KJV: 9:2 Then shalt thou vnderstand, that it is the very same time, wherein the highest will begin to visite the world which he made.
text.NRSV: “then you will know that it is the very time when the Most High is about to visit the world that he has made.”
text.DRB: then shalt thou understand, that the same is the time wherein the Highest will begin to visit the world that was made by him.
And when there shall be seen in the world moving of places, and trouble of peoples,
text.KJV: 9:3 Therefore when there shall bee seene earthquakes and vprores of the people in the world:
text.NRSV: So when there shall appear in the world earthquakes, tumult of peoples, intrigues of nations, wavering of leaders, confusion of princes,

text.DRB: then shalt thou understand, that of these spake the Highest from the days that were before thee, from the beginning.
text.KJV: 9:4 Then shalt thou wel vnderstand, that the most high spake of those things from the dayes that were before thee, euen from the beginning.
text.NRSV: then you will know that it was of these that the Most High spoke from the days that were of old, from the beginning.

text.DRB: For as all that is made in the world hath a beginning, and also a consummation, and the consummation is manifest:
text.KJV: 9:5 For like as all that is made in the world hath a beginning, and an ende, and the end is manifest:
text.NRSV: For just as with everything that has occurred in the world, the beginning is evident,] and the end manifest;

text.DRB: so also the times of the Highest have the beginning manifest in wonders and powers, and the consummations in work and in signs.
text.KJV: 9:6 Euen so the times also of the highest, haue plaine beginnings in wonders and powerfull workes, and endings in effects and signes.
text.NRSV: so also are the times of the Most High: the beginnings are manifest in wonders and mighty works, and the end in penalties] and in signs.

text.DRB: And it shall be, every one that shall be saved, and that can escape by his works, and by faith, in which you have believed,
text.KJV: 9:7 And euery one that shalbe saued, and shalbe able to escape by his works, and by faith, whereby ye haue beleeued,
text.NRSV: ‘It shall be that all who will be saved and will be able to escape on account of their works, or on account of the faith by which they have believed,

text.DRB: shall be left out of the foresaid dangers, and shall see my salvation in my land, and in my coasts, because I have sanctified myself from the world.
text.KJV: 9:8 Shall be preserued from the sayd perils, and shall see my saluation, in my land, and within my borders: for I haue sanctified them for me, from the beginning.
text.NRSV: will survive the dangers that have been predicted, and will see my salvation in my land and within my borders, which I have sanctified for myself from the beginning.

text.DRB: good works are means to salvation. And he answered me, and said: Measuring measure thou the time in it-self: and it shall be when thou seest, after a certain part of the signs which are spoken of before shall pass,
text.KJV: 9:9 Then shall they be in pitifull case which now haue abused my wayes: and they that haue cast them away desspitefully, shall dwell in torments.
text.NRSV: Then those who have now abused my ways shall be amazed, and those who have rejected them with contempt shall live in torments.

text.DRB: For they that knew not me, having obtained benefits when they lived:
text.KJV: 9:10 For such, as in their life haue receiued benefits, & haue not knowen me:
text.NRSV: For as many as did not acknowledge me in their lifetime, though they received my benefits,

text.DRB: and they that loathed my law, when they yet had liberty,
text.KJV: 9:11 And they that haue loathed my law, while they had yet liberty, and when as yet place of repentance was open vnto them, vnderstood not, but despised it:
text.NRSV: and as many as scorned my law while they still had freedom, and did not understand but despised it] while an opportunity of repentance was still open to them,

text.DRB: and when as yet place of penance was open to them understood not, but despised: they must after death in torment know it.
text.KJV: 9:12 The same must know it after death by paine.
text.NRSV: these must in torment acknowledge it] after death.

text.DRB: Thou therefore be not yet curious, how the impious shall be tormented: but inquire how the just shall be saved, and whose the world is, and for whom the world is, & when.
text.KJV: 9:13 And therefore be thou not curious, how the vngodly shalbe punished and when: but enquire how the righteous shall be saued, whose the world is, and for whom the world is created.
text.NRSV: Therefore, do not continue to be curious about how the ungodly will be punished; but inquire how the righteous will be saved, those to whom the age belongs and for whose sake the age was made.’]

text.DRB: And I answered, and said:
text.KJV: 9:14 Then answered I, and said,
text.NRSV: I answered and said,

text.DRB: I have spoken heretofore, and now I say, and hereafter will say: that they are more which perish than that shall be saved: .
text.KJV: 9:15 I haue said before, and now doe speake, and will speake it also heereafter: that there be many moe of them which perish, then of them which shall be saued,
text.NRSV: ‘I said before, and I say now, and will say it again: there are more who perish than those who will be saved,

text.DRB: as a flood is multiplied above, more than a drop.
text.KJV: 9:16 Like as a waue is greater then a droppe.
text.NRSV: as a wave is greater than a drop of water.’

text.DRB: And he answered me, and said: Like as the field so also the seeds: and as the flowers, such also the colours: and as the workman, such also the work, and such as the husbandman, such is the husbandry: because it was the time of the world.
text.KJV: 9:17 And he answered me, saying: like as the field is, so is also the seed: as the flowres be, such are the colours also: such as the workeman is, such also is the worke: and as the husbandman is himselfe, so is his husbandry also: for it was the time of the world.
text.NRSV: He answered me and said, ‘As is the field, so is the seed; and as are the flowers, so are the colours; and as is the work, so is the product; and as is the farmer, so is the threshing-floor.

text.DRB: And now when I was preparing for them, for these that now are before the world was made, wherein they should dwell: and no man gainsaid me.
text.KJV: 9:18 And now when I prepared the world, which was not yet made, euen for them to dwell in that now liue, no man spake against me.
text.NRSV: For there was a time in this age when I was preparing for those who now exist, before the world was made for them to live in, and no one opposed me then, for no one existed;

text.DRB: For then every man, and now the creator in this world prepared, and harvest not failing, and law unsearchable their manners are corrupted.
text.KJV: 9:19 For then euery one obeyed, but now the maners of them which are created in this world that is made, are corrupted by a perpetuall seed, & by a law which is vnsearchable, rid themselues.
text.NRSV: but now those who have been created in this world, which is supplied both with an unfailing table and an inexhaustible pasture,] have become corrupt in their ways.

text.DRB: And I considered the world, and behold there was danger because of the cogitations that came in it.
text.KJV: 9:20 So I considered the world, and behold there was perill, because of the deuices that were come into it.
text.NRSV: So I considered my world, and saw that it was lost. I saw that my earth was in peril because of the devices of those who] had come into it.

text.DRB: And I saw, and spared it very much: and I kept unto myself a grape kernel of a cluster, and a plant of a great tribe.
text.KJV: 9:21 And I saw and spared it greatly, and haue kept me a grape of the cluster, and a plant of a great people.
text.NRSV: And I saw and spared some] with great difficulty, and saved for myself one grape out of a cluster, and one plant out of a great forest.]

text.DRB: Let the multitude therefore perish, which was born without cause, and let my kernel be kept, and my plant: because I finished it with much labour.
text.KJV: 9:22 Let the multitude perish then, which was borne in vaine, and let my grape be kept and my plant: for with great labour haue I made it perfect.
text.NRSV: So let the multitude perish that has been born in vain, but let my grape and my plant be saved, because with much labour I have perfected them.

text.DRB: And thou if thou add yet seven other days, but thou shalt not fast in them,
text.KJV: 9:23 Neuerthelesse if thou wilt cease yet seuen dayes moe (but thou shalt not fast in them.)
text.NRSV: ‘Now, if you will let seven days more pass—do not, however, fast during them,

text.DRB: thou shalt go into a field of flowers, where no house is built: and thou shalt eat only of the flowers of the field, and flesh thou shalt not taste, and wine thou shalt not drink, but only flowers.
text.KJV: 9:24 But goe into a field of flowres, where no house is builded, and eate only the flowres of the field, Tast no flesh, drinke no wine, but eate flowres onely.
text.NRSV: but go into a field of flowers where no house has been built, and eat only of the flowers of the field, and taste no meat and drink no wine, but eat only flowers—

text.DRB: Pray to the Highest without intermission, and I will come, and will speak with thee.
text.KJV: 9:25 And pray vnto the Highest continually, then wil I come and talke with thee.
text.NRSV: and pray to the Most High continually, then I will come and talk with you.’

text.DRB: And I went forth, as he said to me, into a field which is called Ardath, and I sat there among the flowers. And I did eat of the herbs of the field, and the meat of them made me full.
text.KJV: 9:26 So I went my way into the field which is called Ardath, like as he commanded me, and there I sate amongst the flowres, and did eate of the herbes of the field, and the meate of the same satisfied me.
text.NRSV: So I went, as he directed me, into the field that is called Ardat;] there I sat among the flowers and ate of the plants of the field, and the nourishment they afforded satisfied me.

text.DRB: And it came to pass after seven days, and I sat down upon the grass, and my heart was troubled again as before.
text.KJV: 9:27 After seuen dayes I sate vpon the grasse, and my heart was vexed within me, like as before.
text.NRSV: After seven days, while I lay on the grass, my heart was troubled again as it was before.

text.DRB: And my mouth was opened, and I began to speak before the Highest, and said:
text.KJV: 9:28 And I opened my mouth, and beganne to talke before the most High and said,
text.NRSV: Then my mouth was opened, and I began to speak before the Most High, and said,

text.DRB: O Lord thou shewing thyself to us, wast shewed to our fathers in the desert, which is not trodden, and unfruitful, when they came out of Ægypt: and saying thou saidst: Exod. 19. & 24. .
text.KJV: 9:29 O Lord, thou that shewest thy selfe vnto vs, thou wast shewed vnto our fathers in the wildernesse, in a place where no man treadeth, in a barren place when they came out of Egypt.
text.NRSV: ‘O Lord, you showed yourself among us, to our ancestors in the wilderness when they came out from Egypt and when they came into the untrodden and unfruitful wilderness;

text.DRB: Thou Israel hear me, and seed of Jacob attend to my words.
text.KJV: 9:30 And thou spakest, saying, Heare me, O Israel, and marke my words, thou seed of Iacob.
text.NRSV: and you said, “Hear me, O Israel, and give heed to my words, O descendants of Jacob.

text.DRB: For behold, I sow my law in you, and it shall bring forth fruit in you, and you shall be glorified in it for ever.
text.KJV: 9:31 For behold I sow my law in you, and it shall bring fruite in you, and yee shall be honoured in it for euer.
text.NRSV: For I sow my law in you, and it shall bring forth fruit in you, and you shall be glorified through it for ever.”

text.DRB: For our fathers receiving the law observed it not, and kept not my ordinances, and the fruit of the law did not appear: for it could not, because it was thine.
text.KJV: 9:32 But our fathers which receiued the law, kept it not, and obserued not thy ordinances, and though the fruite of thy law did not perish, neither could it, for it was thine:
text.NRSV: But though our ancestors received the law, they did not keep it and did not observe the] statutes; yet the fruit of the law did not perish—for it could not, because it was yours.

text.DRB: For they that received it, perished, not keeping that which had been sown in them.
text.KJV: 9:33 Yet they that receiued it, perished, because they kept not the thing that was sowen in them.
text.NRSV: Yet those who received it perished, because they did not keep what had been sown in them.

text.DRB: And behold it is the custom, that when the earth hath received seed, or the sea a ship, or some vessel meat or drink: when that shall be destroyed wherein it was sown, or into the which it was cast:
text.KJV: 9:34 And loe, it is a custome when the ground hath receiued seed, or the Sea a ship, or any vessel, meate or drinke, that, that being perished wherein it was sowen, or cast into,
text.NRSV: Now this is the general rule that, when the ground has received seed, or the sea a ship, or any dish food or drink, and when it comes about that what was sown or what was launched or what was put in is destroyed,

text.DRB: that which was sown, or cast in, or the things that were received, are destroyed withal, and the things received now tarry not with us: but it is not so done to us.
text.KJV: 9:35 That thing also which was sowen or cast therein, or receiued, doth perish, and remaineth not with vs: but with vs it hath not happened so.
text.NRSV: they are destroyed, but the things that held them remain; yet with us it has not been so.

text.DRB: We indeed that received the law, sinning have perished, and our heart that received it:
text.KJV: 9:36 For we that haue receiued the law perish by sinne, and our heart also which receiued it.
text.NRSV: For we who have received the law and sinned will perish, as well as our hearts that received it;

text.KJV: 9:37 Notwithstanding the law perisheth not, but remaineth in his force.
text.NRSV: “We who have received the law and sinned will perish, as well as our heart which received it;”
text.DRB: For the law hath not perished, but hath remained in his labour. Esai. 48.

text.KJV: 9:38 And when I spake these things in my heart, I looked backe with mine eyes, & vpon the right side I saw a woman, and behold, she mourned, & wept with a loud voyce, and was much grieued in heart, and her clothes were rent, and she had ashes vpon her head.
text.NRSV: “the law, however, does not perish but remains in its glory.”
text.DRB: And when I spake these things in my heart, I looked back with mine eyes, and saw a woman on the right side, and behold she mourned, and wept with a loud voice, and was sorrowful in mind exceedingly, and her garments rent, and ashes upon her head.
And I left the cogitations, wherein I was thinking, and I turned to her and said to her:
text.KJV: 9:39 Then let I my thoughts goe that I was in, and turned me vnto her,
text.NRSV: Then I dismissed the thoughts with which I had been engaged, and turned to her

text.DRB: Why weepest thou? and why art thou sorry in mind. And she said to me:
text.KJV: 9:40 And said vnto her, Wherefore weepest thou? why art thou so grieued in thy minde?
text.NRSV: and said to her, ‘Why are you weeping, and why are you grieved at heart?’

text.DRB: Suffer me my Lord, that I may lament myself, and add sorrow: because I am of a very pensive mind, and am humbled exceedingly.
text.KJV: 9:41 And she said vnto me, Sir, let me alone, that I may bewaile my selfe, and adde vnto my sorow, for I am sore vexed in my minde, and brought very low.
text.NRSV: She said to me, ‘Let me alone, my lord, so that I may weep for myself and continue to mourn, for I am greatly embittered in spirit and deeply distressed.’

text.DRB: And I said to her: What aileth thee: tell me. And she said to me:
text.KJV: 9:42 And I said vnto her, What aileth thee? Tell me.
text.NRSV: I said to her, ‘What has happened to you? Tell me.’

text.DRB: I thy servant have been barren, and have not born child, having a husband thirty years.
text.KJV: 9:43 She said vnto me, I thy seruant haue bene barren, and had no childe, though I had an husband thirty yeres.
text.NRSV: And she said to me, ‘Your servant was barren and had no child, though I lived with my husband for thirty years.

text.DRB: For I every hour, and every day, and these thirty years do beseech the Highest night and day.
text.KJV: 9:44 And those thirtie yeeres I did nothing else day and night, and euery houre, but make my prayer to þ highest.
text.NRSV: Every hour and every day during those thirty years I prayed to the Most High, night and day.

text.DRB: And it came to pass, after thirty years God heard me thy handmaid, and saw my humility, and attended to my tribulation, and gave me a son: and I was very joyful upon him, and my husband, and all my citizens, and we did glorify the Strong exceedingly.
text.KJV: 9:45 After thirtie yeeres, God heard me thine handmaid, looked vpon my misery, considered my trouble, and gaue me a sonne: and I was very glad of him, so was my husband also, and all my neighbours, and we gaue great honour vnto the Almightie.
text.NRSV: And after thirty years God heard your servant, and looked upon my low estate, and considered my distress, and gave me a son. I rejoiced greatly over him, I and my husband and all my neighbours;] and we gave great glory to the Mighty One.

text.DRB: And I nourished him with much labour.
text.KJV: 9:46 And I nourished him with great trauaile.
text.NRSV: And I brought him up with much care.

text.DRB: And it came to pass when he was grown, and came to take a wife, I made a feast day.
text.KJV: 9:47 So when he grew vp, and came to the time that he should haue a wife, I made a feast.
text.NRSV: So when he grew up and I came to take a wife for him, I set a day for the marriage feast.

text.DRB: and we all overthrew the lights, and all my citizens rose up to comfort me, and I was quiet until the other day at night.
text.KJV: 10:2 Then we all ouerthrew the lights, and all my neighbours rose vp to comfort me, so I tooke my rest vnto the second day at night.
text.NRSV: So all of us put out our lamps, and all my neighbours] attempted to console me; I remained quiet until the evening of the second day.

text.DRB: And it came to pass, when all were quiet to comfort me, that I might be quiet: and I arose in the night, and fled: and came as thou seest into this field.
text.KJV: 10:3 And it came to passe when they had all left off to comfort me, to the end I might be quiet: then rose I vp by night and fled, and came hither into this field, as thou seest.
text.NRSV: But when all of them had stopped consoling me, encouraging me to be quiet, I got up in the night and fled, and I came to this field, as you see.

text.DRB: And I mean now not to return into the city, but to stay here: and neither to eat, nor drink, but without intermission to mourn, and to fast until I die.
text.KJV: 10:4 And I doe now purpose not to returne into the citie, but here to stay, and neither to eate nor drinke, but continually to mourne, & to fast vntil I die.
text.NRSV: And now I intend not to return to the town, but to stay here; I will neither eat nor drink, but will mourn and fast continually until I die.’

text.DRB: And I left the talk wherein I was, and with anger answered her, and said:
text.KJV: 10:5 Then left I the meditations wherein I was, and spake to her in anger, saying,
text.NRSV: Then I broke off the reflections with which I was still engaged, and answered her in anger and said,

text.DRB: Thou fool above all woman, seest thou not our mourning, & what things chance to us?
text.KJV: 10:6 Thou foolish woman aboue all other, seest thou not our mourning, and what happeneth vnto vs?
text.NRSV: ‘You most foolish of women, do you not see our mourning, and what has happened to us?

text.DRB: Because Sion our mother is sorrowful with all sorrow, and humbled, and mourneth most bitterly.
text.KJV: 10:7 How that Sion our mother is full of all heauinesse, and much humbled, mourning very sore?
text.NRSV: For Zion, the mother of us all, is in deep grief and great distress.

text.DRB: And now whereas we all mourn, and are sad: whereas we are sorrowful, & art thou sorrowful for one son?
text.KJV: 10:8 And now seeing we all mourne, and are sad, for we are all in heauinesse, art thou grieued for one sonne?
text.NRSV: It is most appropriate to mourn now, because we are all mourning, and to be sorrowful, because we are all sorrowing; you are sorrowing for one son, but we, the whole world, for our mother.]

text.DRB: For ask the earth, and it will tell thee: that it is she, that ought to lament the fall of so many things that spring upon it.
text.KJV: 10:9 For aske the earth, and she shall tell thee, that it is she, which ought to mourne, for the fall of so many that grow vpon her.
text.NRSV: Now ask the earth, and she will tell you that it is she who ought to mourn over so many who have come into being upon her.

text.DRB: And of her were all born from the beginning, and others shall come: and behold, almost all walk into perdition, and the multitude of them cometh to destruction.
text.KJV: 10:10 For out of her came all at the first, and out of her shal all others come: and behold they walke almost all into destruction, and a multitude of them is vtterly rooted out.
text.NRSV: From the beginning all have been born of her, and others will come; and, lo, almost all go] to perdition, and a multitude of them will come to doom.

text.DRB: And who then ought to mourn more, but she that hath lost so great a multitude, rather than thou which art sorry for one?
text.KJV: 10:11 Who then should make more mourning, then she that hath lost so great a multitude, and not thou which art sory but for one?
text.NRSV: Who then ought to mourn the more, she who lost so great a multitude, or you who are grieving for one alone?

text.DRB: And if thou say unto me, that my mourning is not like the earth's: because I have lost the fruit of my womb, which I bare with sorrows, and brought forth with pains:
text.KJV: 10:12 But if thou sayest vnto me, My lamentation is not like the earths, because I haue lost the fruit of my womb, which I brought foorth with paines, and bare with sorrowes.
text.NRSV: But if you say to me, “My lamentation is not like the earth’s, for I have lost the fruit of my womb, which I brought forth in pain and bore in sorrow;

text.DRB: but the earth according to the manner of the earth, and the present multitude in it hath departed as it came: and I say to thee,
text.KJV: 10:13 But the earth not so: for the multitude present in it, according to the course of the earth, is gone, as it came.
text.NRSV: but it is with the earth according to the way of the earth—the multitude that is now in it goes as it came”;

text.DRB: as thou hast brought forth with pain, so the earth also giveth her fruit for man from the beginning to him that made her.
text.KJV: 10:14 Then say I vnto thee, Like as thou hast brought foorth with labour: euen so the earth also hath giuen her fruit, namely man, euer sithence the beginning, vnto him that made her.
text.NRSV: then I say to you, “Just as you brought forth in sorrow, so the earth also has from the beginning given her fruit, that is, humankind, to him who made her.”

text.DRB: Now therefore keep in with thy sorrow, and bear stoutly the chances that have befallen thee.
text.KJV: 10:15 Now therefore keepe thy sorrow to thy selfe, and beare with a good courage that which hath befallen thee.
text.NRSV: Now, therefore, keep your sorrow to yourself, and bear bravely the troubles that have come upon you.

text.DRB: For if thou justify the end of God, thou shalt in time both receive his counsel, and also in such things thou shalt be praised.
text.KJV: 10:16 For if thou shalt acknowledge the determination of God to be iust, thou shalt both receiue thy sonne in time, and shalt be commended amongst women.
text.NRSV: For if you acknowledge the decree of God to be just, you will receive your son back in due time, and will be praised among women.

text.DRB: Go in therefore into the city to thy husband. And she said to me:
text.KJV: 10:17 Goe thy way then into the citie, to thine husband.
text.NRSV: Therefore go into the town to your husband.’

text.DRB: I will not do it, neither will I enter into the city, but here will I die.
text.KJV: 10:18 And she said vnto me, That will I not doe: I will not goe into the city, but here will I die.
text.NRSV: She said to me, ‘I will not do so; I will not go into the city, but I will die here.’

text.DRB: And I added yet to speak to her, and said:
text.KJV: 10:19 So I proceeded to speake further vnto her, and said,
text.NRSV: So I spoke again to her, and said,

text.DRB: Do not this word, but consent to him that counseleth thee. For how many are the chances of Sion? Take comfort for the sorrow of Jerusalem.
text.KJV: 10:20 Doe not so, but bee counselled by me: for how many are the aduersities of Sion? Bee comforted in regard of the sorow of Ierusalem.
text.NRSV: ‘Do not do that, but let yourself be persuaded—for how many are the adversities of Zion?—and be consoled because of the sorrow of Jerusalem.

text.DRB: For thou seest that our sanctification is made
text.KJV: 10:21 For thou seest that our Sanctuary is laid waste, our Altar broken downe, our Temple destroyed.
text.NRSV: For you see how our sanctuary has been laid waste, our altar thrown down, our temple destroyed;

text.DRB: and our psalter is humbled, and hymn is silent, and our exultation is dissolved, and the light of our candlestick is extinguished, and the ark of our testament is taken for spoil, and our holy things are contaminated, and the name that is invocated upon us is almost profaned: and our children have suffered contumely, and our Priests are burnt, and our Levites are gone into captivity, and our virgins are deflowered, and our wives have suffered rape, and our just men are violently taken, and our little ones are lost, and our young men are in bondage, and our valiants are made impotent:
text.KJV: 10:22 Our Psaltery is laid on þ ground, our song is put to silence, our reioycing is at an end, the light of our candlesticke is put out, the Arke of our Couenant is spoiled, our holy things are defiled, and the Name that is called vpon vs, is almost prophaned: our children are put to shame, our priests are burnt, our Leuites are gone into captiuitie, our virgines are defiled, and our wiues rauished, our righteous men caried away, our litle ones destroyed, our yong men are brought in bondage, and our strong men are become weake.
text.NRSV: our harp has been laid low, our song has been silenced, and our rejoicing has been ended; the light of our lampstand has been put out, the ark of our covenant has been plundered, our holy things have been polluted, and the name by which we are called has been almost profaned; our children] have suffered abuse, our priests have been burned to death, our Levites have gone into exile, our virgins have been defiled, and our wives have been ravished; our righteous men] have been carried off, our little ones have been cast out, our young men have been enslaved and our strong men made powerless.

text.DRB: and that which is greatest of all, the seal of Sion, because she is unsealed of her glory: For she is also delivered into the hands of them that hate us.
text.KJV: 10:23 And which is the greatest of all, the seale of Sion hath now lost her honour: for she is deliuered into the hands of them that hate vs.
text.NRSV: And, worst of all, the seal of Zion has been deprived of its glory, and given over into the hands of those that hate us.

text.DRB: Thou therefore shake off thy great heaviness, and lay away from thee the multitude of sorrows, that the Strong may be propitious to thee again, and the Highest will give thee rest, rest from thy labours.
text.KJV: 10:24 And therefore shake off thy great heauinesse, and put away the multitude of sorrowes, that the mighty may be mercifull vnto thee againe, and the highest shal giue thee rest, and ease from thy labour.
text.NRSV: Therefore shake off your great sadness and lay aside your many sorrows, so that the Mighty One may be merciful to you again, and the Most High may give you rest, a respite from your troubles.’

text.KJV: 10:25 And it came to passe while I was talking with her, behold her face vpon a sudden shined exceedingly, & her countenance glistered, so that I was afraid of her, and mused what it might be.
text.NRSV: “While I was speaking to her, her face suddenly began to shine exceedingly; her countenance flashed like lightning, so that I was too frightened to approach her.”
text.DRB: And it came to pass, when I spake to her, her face did shine suddenly, and her shape, and her visage was made glistering, so that I was afraid exceedingly at her, and thought what this thing should be.
And behold, suddenly she put forth a great sound of a voice full of fear, that the earth was moved at the woman's sound. And I saw:
text.KJV: 10:26 And behold suddenly, she made a great cry very fearful: so that the earth shooke at the noise of the woman.
text.NRSV: she suddenly uttered a loud and fearful cry, so that the earth shook at the sound.

text.KJV: 10:27 And I looked, and beholde, the woman appeared vnto me no more, but there was a city builded, and a large place shewed it selfe from the foundations: then was I afraid, and cried with a lowd voice, and said,
text.NRSV: “And I looked, and behold, the woman was no longer visible to me, but there was a city being built, and a place of huge foundations showed itself.”
text.DRB: and behold, the woman did no more appear unto me, but a city was built, & a place was shewed of great foundations: and I was afraid, and crying with a loud voice I said:
Where is Uriel the Angel that from the beginning came to me? For he made me come in multitude in excess of this mind, & my end is made into corruption, & my prayer into reproach.
text.KJV: 10:28 Where is Uriel the Angel, who came vnto mee at the first? for hee hath caused me to fall into many traunces, and mine end is turned into corruption, and my prayer to rebuke.
text.NRSV: ‘Where is the angel Uriel, who came to me at first? For it was he who brought me into this overpowering bewilderment; my end has become corruption, and my prayer a reproach.’

text.DRB: And when I was speaking these things, behold he came to me, and saw me.
text.KJV: 10:29 And as I was speaking these wordes, behold, he came vnto me, and looked vpon me.
text.NRSV: While I was speaking these words, the angel who had come to me at first came to me, and when he saw me

text.DRB: And behold I was laid as dead, and my understanding was alienated, and he held my right hand, and strengthened me and set me upon my feet, and said to me:
text.KJV: 10:30 And loe, I lay as one that had bene dead, & mine vnderstanding was taken from me, and he tooke me by the right hand, and comforted mee, and set me vpon my feet, and said vnto me,
text.NRSV: lying there like a corpse, deprived of my understanding, he grasped my right hand and strengthened me and set me on my feet, and said to me,

text.DRB: What aileth thee? And why is thy understanding, and the sense of thy heart troubled, and why art thou troubled? And I said?:
text.KJV: 10:31 What aileth thee? and why art thou so disquieted, and why is thine vnderstanding troubled, & the thoughts of thine heart?
text.NRSV: ‘What is the matter with you? And why are you troubled? And why are your understanding and the thoughts of your mind troubled?’

text.DRB: Because thou hast forsaken me, and I indeed have done according to thy words, and went out into the field: and behold, I have seen, and do see that which I cannot utter. And he said to me:
text.KJV: 10:32 And I said, because thou hast forsaken me, and yet I did according to thy words, and I went into the field, and loe I haue seene, and yet see, that I am not able to expresse.
text.NRSV: I said, ‘It was because you abandoned me. I did as you directed, and went out into the field, and lo, what I have seen, and can still see, I am unable to explain.’

text.DRB: Stand like a man, and I will move thee. And I said:
text.KJV: 10:33 And hee said vnto me, Stand vp manfully, and I wil aduise thee.
text.NRSV: He said to me, ‘Stand up like a man, and I will instruct you.’

text.DRB: Speak thou my Lord in me, forsake me not, that I die not in vain:
text.KJV: 10:34 Then said I, Speake on, my lord in me, onely forsake me not, lest I die frustrate of my hope.
text.NRSV: I said, ‘Speak, my lord; only do not forsake me, so that I may not die before my time.]

text.DRB: because I have seen things that I knew not, and I do hear things that I know not.
text.KJV: 10:35 For I haue seene, that I knew not, and heare that I do not know.
text.NRSV: For I have seen what I did not know, and I hear] what I do not understand

text.DRB: Or is my sense deceived, and doth my soul dream?
text.KJV: 10:36 Or, is my sense deceiued, or my soule in a dreame?
text.NRSV: —or is my mind deceived, and my soul dreaming?

text.DRB: Now therefore I beseech thee, that thou shew unto thy servant concerning this trance. And he answered me, and said:
text.KJV: 10:37 Now therfore, I beseech thee, that thou wilt shew thy seruant of this visiō.
text.NRSV: Now therefore, I beg you to give your servant an explanation of this bewildering vision.’

text.DRB: Hear me, and I will teach thee, and will tell thee of what things thou art afraid: because the Highest hath revealed unto thee many mysteries.
text.KJV: 10:38 He answered me then, & said, Heare me, and I shall enforme thee, and tell thee wherefore thou art afraid: for the highest will reueile many secret things vnto thee.
text.NRSV: He answered me and said, ‘Listen to me, and I will teach you, and tell you about the things that you fear; for the Most High has revealed many secrets to you.

text.DRB: He hath seen thy right way, that without intermission thou was sorrowful for thy people, and didst mourn exceedingly for Sion.
text.KJV: 10:39 Hee hath seene that thy way is right: for that thou sorrowest continually for thy people, and makest great lamentation for Sion.
text.NRSV: He has seen your righteous conduct, and that you have sorrowed continually for your people and mourned greatly over Zion.

text.DRB: This therefore is the understanding of the vision which appeared to thee a little before.
text.KJV: 10:40 This therefore is the meaning of the vision which thou lately sawest.
text.NRSV: This therefore is the meaning of the vision.

text.DRB: The woman whom thou sawest mourning, thou beganst to comfort her.
text.KJV: 10:41 Thou sawest a woman mourning, and thou beganst to comfort her:
text.NRSV: The woman who appeared to you a little while ago, whom you saw mourning and whom you began to console

text.DRB: And now thou seest not the form of the woman, but there appeared to thee a city to be built.
text.KJV: 10:42 But now seest thou the likenesse of the woman no more, but there appeared vnto thee a city builded.
text.NRSV: (you do not now see the form of a woman, but there appeared to you a city being built)]

text.DRB: And because she told thee of the fall of her son, this is the interpretation.
text.KJV: 10:43 And whereas she told thee of the death of her sonne, this is the solution.
text.NRSV: and who told you about the misfortune of her son—this is the interpretation:

text.KJV: 10:44 This woman whom thou sawest, is Sion: and whereas she said vnto thee (euen she whom thou seest as a city builded.)
text.NRSV: “The woman whom you saw is Zion, which you now behold as a city being built.”
text.DRB: This woman which thou sawest, she is Sion, and whereas she told thee of her, whom now also thou shalt see, as a city builded.
And whereas she told thee, that she was barren thirty years: for the which there were thirty years, when there was not yet oblation offered in it.
text.KJV: 10:45 Whereas I say, she said vnto thee, that she hath bene thirty yeres barren: those are the thirty yeeres wherein there was no offering made in her.
text.NRSV: And as for her telling you that she was barren for thirty years, the reason is that there were three thousand] years in the world before any offering was offered in it.]

text.DRB: And it came to pass after thirty years, Salomon built the city, and offered oblations: then it was, when the barren bare a child.
text.KJV: 10:46 But after thirtie yeeres, Solomon builded the city, & offered offrings: and then bare the barren a sonne.
text.NRSV: And after three thousand] years Solomon built the city, and offered offerings; then it was that the barren woman bore a son.

text.DRB: And that which she said unto thee, that she nourished him with labour, this was the habitation in Jerusalem.
text.KJV: 10:47 And whereas she told thee that shee nourished him with labour: that was the dwelling in Hierusalem.
text.NRSV: And as for her telling you that she brought him up with much care, that was the period of residence in Jerusalem.

text.DRB: And whereas she said to thee, that my son coming into the bride chamber died, and that a fall chanced unto him, this was the ruin of Jerusalem that is made.
text.KJV: 10:48 But whereas she said vnto thee, That my sonne comming into his marriage chamber, happened to haue a fall, and died, this was the destruction that came to Hierusalem.
text.NRSV: And as for her saying to you, “My son died as he entered his wedding-chamber”, and that misfortune had overtaken her,] this was the destruction that befell Jerusalem.

text.DRB: And behold, thou hast seen the similitude of her: and because she lamented her son, thou beganst to comfort her: and of these things that have chanced, these were to be opened to thee.
text.KJV: 10:49 And behold, thou sawest her likenesse, and because she mourned for her sonne, thou beganst to comfort her, and of these things which haue chaunced, these are to be opened vnto thee.
text.NRSV: So you saw her likeness, how she mourned for her son, and you began to console her for what had happened.]

text.DRB: And now the Highest seeth that thou wast sorry from the heart: and because with thy whole heart thou sufferest for her, he hath shewed thee the clearness of her glory, and the fairness of her beauty.
text.KJV: 10:50 For now the most High seeth, that thou art grieued vnfainedly, & sufferest from thy whole heart for her, so hath he shewed thee the brightnes of her glory, and the comelinesse of her beautie.
text.NRSV: For now the Most High, seeing that you are sincerely grieved and profoundly distressed for her, has shown you the brilliance of her glory, and the loveliness of her beauty.

text.DRB: For therefore did he say to thee, that thou shouldest tarry in a field where house is not built.
text.KJV: 10:51 And therfore I bad thee remaine in þ field, where no house was builded.
text.NRSV: Therefore I told you to remain in the field where no house had been built,

text.DRB: For I knew that the Highest began to shew thee these things:
text.KJV: 10:52 For I knew that the Highest would shew this vnto thee.
text.NRSV: for I knew that the Most High would reveal these things to you.

text.DRB: therefore I said unto thee, that thou shouldest go
text.KJV: 10:53 Therefore I commanded thee to goe into the field, where no foundation of any building was.
text.NRSV: Therefore I told you to go into the field where there was no foundation of any building,

text.DRB: For the work of man's building could not be born in the place, where the city of the Highest began to be shewed.
text.KJV: 10:54 For in the place wherein the Highest beginneth to shew his city, ther can no mans building be able to stand.
text.NRSV: because no work of human construction could endure in a place where the city of the Most High was to be revealed.

text.DRB: Thou therefore fear not, neither let thy heart dread: but go in, and see the beauty, and greatness of the building, as much as the sight of thine eyes is capable to see:
text.KJV: 10:55 And therfore feare not, let not thy heart be afrighted, but goe thy way in, and see the beautie and greatnesse of the building, as much as thine eyes be able to see:
text.NRSV: ‘Therefore do not be afraid, and do not let your heart be terrified; but go in and see the splendour or] the vastness of the building, as far as it is possible for your eyes to see it,

text.DRB: and afterward thou shalt hear as much, as the hearing of thine ears is capable to hear.
text.KJV: 10:56 And then shalt thou heare as much as thine eares may comprehend.
text.NRSV: and afterwards you will hear as much as your ears can hear.

text.DRB: For thou art blessed above many, and art called with the Highest as few.
text.KJV: 10:57 For thou art blessed aboue many other, and art called with the highest, and so are but few.
text.NRSV: For you are more blessed than many, and you have been called to be with] the Most High as few have been.

text.DRB: And tomorrow night thou shalt tarry here.
text.KJV: 10:58 But to morrow at night thou shalt remaine here.
text.NRSV: But tomorrow night you shall remain here,

text.DRB: and the Highest will shew thee those visions of the things on high, which the Highest will do to them that inhabit upon the earth in the later days.
text.KJV: 10:59 And so shall the highest shew thee visions of the high things, which the most high will do vnto them, that dwel vpon earth in the last dayes. So I slept that night and another, like as he commanded me.
text.NRSV: So I slept that night and the following one, as he had told me.

text.DRB: And I slept that night, and the other next, as he had said to me.

text.KJV: 11:1 Then saw I a dreame, and beholde, there came vp from the Sea an Eagle, which had twelue feathered wings, & three heads.
text.NRSV: On the second night I had a dream: and behold, there came up from the sea an eagle that had twelve feathered wings and three heads.

text.DRB: And I saw, & behold she spread her wings into all the earth, and all the winds of heaven blew upon her, and were gathered together.
text.KJV: 11:2 And I saw, and behold, she spred her wings ouer all the earth, and all the windes of the ayre blewe on her, and were gathered together.
text.NRSV: I saw it spread its wings over] the whole earth, and all the winds of heaven blew upon it, and the clouds were gathered around it.]

text.DRB: And I saw, and of her feathers sprang contrary feathers, and they became little wings, and small.
text.KJV: 11:3 And I beheld, and out of her feathers there grewe other contrary feathers, and they became little feathers, and small.
text.NRSV: I saw that out of its wings there grew opposing wings; but they became little, puny wings.

text.DRB: For her heads were at rest, and the middle head was greater than the other heads, but she rested with them.
text.KJV: 11:4 But her heads were at rest: the head in the middest was greater then the other, yet rested it with the residue.
text.NRSV: But its heads were at rest; the middle head was larger than the other heads, but it too was at rest with them.

text.DRB: And I saw and behold the eagle flew with her wings, and reigned over the earth, and over them that dwell in it.
text.KJV: 11:5 Moreouer I beheld, and loe, the Eagle flew with her feathers, and reigned vpon earth, and ouer them that dwelt therein.
text.NRSV: Then I saw that the eagle flew with its wings, and it reigned over the earth and over those who inhabit it.

text.DRB: And I saw that all things under heaven were subject to her, and no man gainsaid her, no not one of the creature that is upon the earth.
text.KJV: 11:6 And I saw that all things vnder heauen were subiect vnto her, and no man spake against her, no not one creature vpon earth.
text.NRSV: And I saw how all things under heaven were subjected to it, and no one spoke against it—not a single creature that was on the earth.

text.DRB: And I saw, and behold the eagle rose up upon her talons, and made a voice with her wings, saying:
text.KJV: 11:7 And I beheld, and loe, the Eagle rose vpon her talents, and spake to her feathers, saying,
text.NRSV: Then I saw the eagle rise upon its talons, and it uttered a cry to its wings, saying,

text.DRB: Watch not all together, sleep every one in his place, & watch according to time.
text.KJV: 11:8 Watch not all at once, sleepe euery one in his own place, & watch by course.
text.NRSV: ‘Do not all watch at the same time; let each sleep in its own place, and watch in its turn;

text.DRB: But let the heads be preserved to the last.
text.KJV: 11:9 But let the heads be preserued for the last.
text.NRSV: but let the heads be reserved for the last.’

text.DRB: And I saw, and behold the voice came not out of her heads, but from the midst of her body.
text.KJV: 11:10 And I beheld, and loe, the voice went not out of her heads, but from the middest of her body.
text.NRSV: I looked again and saw that the voice did not come from its heads, but from the middle of its body.

text.DRB: And I numbered her contrary wings, and behold they were eight.
text.KJV: 11:11 And I numbred her contrary feathers, and behold, there were eight of them.
text.NRSV: I counted its rival wings, and there were eight of them.

text.DRB: And I saw, and behold on the right side rose one wing, and reigned over all the earth.
text.KJV: 11:12 And I looked, and behold, on the right side there arose one feather, and reigned ouer all the earth.
text.NRSV: As I watched, one wing on the right side rose up, and it reigned over all the earth.

text.DRB: And it came to pass, when it reigned, an end came to it, and the place thereof appeared not: and the next rose up, and reigned, that held much time.
text.KJV: 11:13 And so it was, that when it reigned, the ende of it came, and the place thereof appeared no more: so the next following stood vp and reigned, and had a great time.
text.NRSV: And after a time its reign came to an end, and it disappeared, so that even its place was no longer visible. Then the next wing rose up and reigned, and it continued to reign a long time.

text.DRB: And it came to pass, when it reigned, and the end of it also came, that it appeared not as the former.
text.KJV: 11:14 And it happened, that when it reigned, the end of it came also, like as the first, so that it appeared no more.
text.NRSV: While it was reigning its end came also, so that it disappeared like the first.

text.DRB: And behold, a voice was sent forth to it, saying:
text.KJV: 11:15 Then came there a voice vnto it, and sayd,
text.NRSV: And a voice sounded, saying to it,

text.DRB: Hear thou that hast held the earth of long time. Thus I tell thee before thou begin not to appear.
text.KJV: 11:16 Heare, thou that hast borne rule ouer the earth so long: this I say vnto thee, before thou beginnest to appeare no more.
text.NRSV: ‘Listen to me, you who have ruled the earth all this time; I announce this to you before you disappear.

text.DRB: None after thee shall hold thy time, no nor the half thereof.
text.KJV: 11:17 There shall none after thee attaine vnto thy time, neither vnto the halfe thereof.
text.NRSV: After you no one shall rule as long as you have ruled, not even half as long.’

text.DRB: And the third lifted up itself, and held the principality as also the former: and that also appeared not.
text.KJV: 11:18 Then arose the third, and reigned as the other before: and appeared no more also.
text.NRSV: Then the third wing raised itself up, and held the rule as the earlier ones had done, and it also disappeared.

text.DRB: And so it chanced to all the other by one and by one to have the principality, and again to appear no-where.
text.KJV: 11:19 So went it with all the residue one after another, as that euery one reigned, and then appeared no more.
text.NRSV: And so it went with all the wings; they wielded power one after another and then were never seen again.

text.DRB: And I saw, and behold in time the rest of the wings were sent up on the right side, that they also might hold the principality: and of them there were that held it, but yet forthwith they appeared not.
text.KJV: 11:20 Then I beheld, & loe, in processe of time, the feathers that folowed, stood vp vpon the right side, that they might rule also, and some of them ruled, but within a while they appeared no more:
text.NRSV: I kept looking, and in due time the wings that followed] also rose up on the right] side, in order to rule. There were some of them that ruled, yet disappeared suddenly;

text.DRB: For some also of them stood up, but they held not the principality.
text.KJV: 11:21 For some of them were set vp, but ruled not.
text.NRSV: and others of them rose up, but did not hold the rule.

text.DRB: And I saw after these things, and behold the twelve wings, and two little wings appeared not:
text.KJV: 11:22 After this I looked, and behold, the twelue feathers appeared no more, nor the two little feathers:
text.NRSV: And after this I looked and saw that the twelve wings and the two little wings had disappeared,

text.DRB: and nothing remained in the body of the eagle but two heads resting, & six little wings.
text.KJV: 11:23 And there was no more vpon the Eagles body, but three heads that rested, and sixe little wings.
text.NRSV: and nothing remained on the eagle’s body except the three heads that were at rest and six little wings.

text.DRB: And I saw, and behold from the six little wings two were divided, and they remained under the head, that is on the right side. For four tarried in their place.
text.KJV: 11:24 Then saw I also that two little feathers diuided themselues from the sixe, and remained vnder the head, that was vpon the right side: for the foure continued in their place.
text.NRSV: As I kept looking I saw that two little wings separated from the six and remained under the head that was on the right side; but four remained in their place.

text.DRB: And I saw, and behold the underwings thought to set up themselves, and to hold the principalities.
text.KJV: 11:25 And I beheld, & loe, the feathers that were vnder the wing, thought to set vp themselues, and to haue the rule.
text.NRSV: Then I saw that these little wings] planned to set themselves up and hold the rule.

text.DRB: And I saw, and behold one was set up, but forthwith it appeared not.
text.KJV: 11:26 And I beheld, & loe, there was one set vp, but shortly it appeared no more.
text.NRSV: As I kept looking, one was set up, but suddenly disappeared;

text.DRB: And they that were second did sooner vanish away than the former.
text.KJV: 11:27 And the second was sooner away then the first.
text.NRSV: a second also, and this disappeared more quickly than the first.

text.DRB: And I saw, and behold the two that remained, thought with themselves that they also would reign:
text.KJV: 11:28 And I beheld, and loe, the two that remained, thought also in themselues to reigne.
text.NRSV: While I continued to look the two that remained were planning between themselves to reign together;

text.DRB: and when they were thinking thereon, behold one of the resting heads, which was the middle one awaked, for this was greater than the other two heads.
text.KJV: 11:29 And when they so thought, behold, there awaked one of the heads that were at rest, namely it that was in the middest, for that was greater then the two other heads.
text.NRSV: and while they were planning, one of the heads that were at rest (the one that was in the middle) suddenly awoke; it was greater than the other two heads.

text.DRB: And behold the head with them that were with him turned, and did eat the two underwings that thought to reign.
text.KJV: 11:31 And behold, the head was turned with them that were with it, and did eate vp the two feathers vnder the wing that would haue reigned.
text.NRSV: and how the head turned with those that were with it and devoured the two little wings] that were planning to reign.

text.DRB: And this head terrified all the earth, & ruled in it over them that inhabit the earth with much labour, and he that held the dominion of the whole world above all the wings that were.
text.KJV: 11:32 But this head put the whole earth in feare, and bare rule in it ouer all those that dwelt vpon the earth, with much oppression, and it had the gouernance of the world more then all the wings that had beene.
text.NRSV: Moreover, this head gained control of the whole earth, and with much oppression dominated its inhabitants; it had greater power over the world than all the wings that had gone before.

text.DRB: And I saw after these things, and behold the middle head suddenly appeared not, as did the wings.
text.KJV: 11:33 And after this I beheld, and loe the head that was in the midst, suddenly appeared no more, like as the wings.
text.NRSV: After this I looked again and saw the head in the middle suddenly disappear, just as the wings had done.

text.DRB: And there remained two heads, which reigned also themselves over the earth, and over them that dwelt therein.
text.KJV: 11:34 But there remained the two heads, which also in like sort ruled vpon the earth, and ouer those that dwelt therein.
text.NRSV: But the two heads remained, which also in like manner ruled over the earth and its inhabitants.

text.DRB: And I saw, and behold the head on the right side devoured that which was on the left.
text.KJV: 11:35 And I beheld, and loe, the head vpon the right side, deuoured it, that was vpon the left side.
text.NRSV: And while I looked, I saw the head on the right side devour the one on the left.

text.DRB: And I heard a voice saying to me, Look against thee, and consider what thou seest.
text.KJV: 11:36 Then I heard a voyce, which said vnto me, Looke before thee, and consider the thing that thou seest.
text.NRSV: Then I heard a voice saying to me, ‘Look in front of you and consider what you see.’

text.KJV: 11:37 And I beheld, and loe, as it were a roaring Lyon, chased out of the wood: and I saw that hee sent out a mans voyce vnto the Eagle, and said,
text.NRSV: I looked, and behold, the lion roused out of the forest and roared; and I heard how he uttered a human voice to the eagle, and spoke, saying:
text.DRB: And I saw, and behold as a lion raised out of the wood roaring: and I saw that he sent out a man's voice to the eagle. And he spake saying.
Hear thou, and I will speak to thee, and the Highest will say to thee:
text.KJV: 11:38 Heare thou, I will talke with thee, and the highest shall say vnto thee,
text.NRSV: ‘Listen and I will speak to you. The Most High says to you,

text.DRB: Is it not thou that hast overcome of the four beasts, which I made to reign in my world, and that by them the end of their times might come?
text.KJV: 11:39 Art not thou it that remainest of the foure beasts, whom I made to raigne in my world, that the end of their times might come through them?
text.NRSV: “Are you not the one that remains of the four beasts that I had made to reign in my world, so that the end of my times might come through them?

text.KJV: 11:40 And the fourth came and ouercame all the beasts that were past, and had power ouer the world with great fearefulnesse, and ouer the whole compasse of the earth with much wicked oppression, and so long time dwelt he vpon the earth with deceit.
text.NRSV: “The fourth has come and has conquered all the creatures that have been before; and has kept the world in his control with much oppression.”
text.DRB: And the fourth coming overcame all the beasts that were past, and by might held the world with much fear, and all the world with most wicked labour, and he inhabited the whole earth so long time with deceit.
And thou hast judged the earth not with truth.
text.KJV: 11:41 For the earth hast thou not iudged with trueth.
text.NRSV: You have judged the earth, but not with truth,

text.DRB: For thou hast afflicted the meek, and hast troubled them that were quiet, and hast loved liars, and hast destroyed their habitations that did fructify, & hast overthrown their walls that did not hurt thee.
text.KJV: 11:42 For thou hast afflicted the meeke, thou hast hurt the peaceable, thou hast loued lyers, and destroyed the dwellings of them that brought forth fruite, and hast cast downe the walles of such, as did thee no harme.
text.NRSV: for you have oppressed the meek and injured the peaceable; you have hated those who tell the truth, and have loved liars; you have destroyed the homes of those who brought forth fruit, and have laid low the walls of those who did you no harm.

text.DRB: And thy contumely is ascended even to the Highest, and thy pride to the Strong.
text.KJV: 11:43 Therefore is thy wrongfull dealing come vp vnto the Highest, and thy pride vnto the Mighty.
text.NRSV: Your insolence has come up before the Most High, and your pride to the Mighty One.

text.DRB: And the Highest hath looked upon the proud times: and behold they are ended, and the abominations thereof are accomplished.
text.KJV: 11:44 The Highest also hath looked vpon the proud times, and behold, they are ended, and his abominations are fulfilled.
text.NRSV: The Most High has looked at his times; now they have ended, and his ages have reached completion.

text.DRB: Therefore thou eagle appear no more, and thy horrible wings and thy little wings most wicked, and thy heads malignant, and thy talons most wicked, and all thy body vain,
text.KJV: 11:45 And therefore appeare no more thou Eagle, nor thy horrible wings, nor thy wicked feathers, nor thy malitious heads, nor thy hurtfull clawes, nor all thy vaine body:
text.NRSV: Therefore you, eagle, will surely disappear, you and your terrifying wings, your most evil little wings, your malicious heads, your most evil talons, and your whole worthless body,

text.DRB: that all the earth may be refreshed, and may return delivered from thy violence, and may hope for his judgement and mercy that made it.
text.KJV: 11:46 That all the earth may be refreshed, and may returne, being deliuered from thy violence, and that she may hope for the iudgement, and mercy of him that made her.
text.NRSV: so that the whole earth, freed from your violence, may be refreshed and relieved, and may hope for the judgement and mercy of him who made it.”’

text.KJV: 12:1 And it came to passe whiles the Lyon spake these words vnto the Eagle, I saw:
text.NRSV: While the lion was saying these words to the eagle, I looked,

text.DRB: and behold the head that had overcome, and those four wings appeared not which passed to him, and were set up to reign: and their reign was small, and full of tumult.
text.KJV: 12:2 And behold, the head that remained, and the foure wings appeared no more, and the two went vnto it, and set themselues vp to raigne, and their kingdome was small and full of vprore.
text.NRSV: and saw that the remaining head had disappeared. The two wings that had gone over to it rose up and] set themselves up to reign, and their reign was brief and full of tumult.

text.KJV: 12:3 And I saw, and behold, they appeared no more, and the whole body of the Eagle was burnt, so that the earth was in great feare: then awaked I out of the trouble and traunce of my minde, and from great feare, and said vnto my spirit,
text.NRSV: and behold, the head that remained and the four wings disappeared, and the whole body of the eagle was burned, and the earth was exceedingly terrified.
text.DRB: And I saw, and behold they appeared not, & all the body of the eagle was burnt, and the earth was afraid exceedingly, & I by the tumult & trance of mind, and for great fear awaked, and said to my spirit:
Behold thou hast given me this, in that, that thou searchest the ways of the Highest.
text.KJV: 12:4 Loe, this hast thou done vnto me, in that thou searchest out the wayes of the Highest.
text.NRSV: ‘You have brought this upon me, because you search out the ways of the Most High.

text.DRB: Behold yet I am weary in mind, and in my spirit I am very feeble, and there is not so much as a little strength in me for the great fear, that I was afraid of this night.
text.KJV: 12:5 Loe, yet am I weary in my mind, and very weake in my spirit: and litle strength is there in me; for the great feare, wherewith I was affrighted this night.
text.NRSV: I am still weary in mind and very weak in my spirit, and not even a little strength is left in me, because of the great fear with which I have been terrified tonight.

text.DRB: Now therefore I will pray the Highest, that he strengthen me even to the end.
text.KJV: 12:6 Therefore wil I now beseech the Highest, that hee will comfort me vnto the end.
text.NRSV: Therefore I will now entreat the Most High that he may strengthen me to the end.’

text.DRB: And I said: Lord Dominatour, if I have found grace before thine eyes, and if I am justified before thee above many, and if indeed my prayer be ascended before thy face,
text.KJV: 12:7 And I said, Lord, that bearest rule, If I haue found grace before thy sight, and if I am iustified with thee, before many others, and if my prayer indeed be come vp before thy face,
text.NRSV: Then I said, ‘O sovereign Lord, if I have found favour in your sight, and if I have been accounted righteous before you beyond many others, and if my prayer has indeed come up before your face,

text.DRB: Strengthen me, and shew unto me thy servant the interpretation, and distinction of this horrible vision, that thou mayst comfort my soul most fully.
text.KJV: 12:8 Comfort me then, and shew me thy seruant the interpretation, and plaine difference of this fearefull vision, that thou maist perfectly comfort my soule.
text.NRSV: strengthen me and show me, your servant, the interpretation and meaning of this terrifying vision so that you may fully comfort my soul.

text.DRB: For thou hast counted me worthy to shew unto me the later times. And he said to me:
text.KJV: 12:9 For thou hast iudged me worthy, to shew me the last times.
text.NRSV: For you have judged me worthy to be shown the end of the times and the last events of the times.’

text.DRB: This is the interpretation of this vision.
text.KJV: 12:10 And he said vnto me, This is the interpretation of the vision.
text.NRSV: He said to me, ‘This is the interpretation of this vision that you have seen:

text.DRB: The eagle which thou sawest coming up from the sea, this is the Kingdom which was seen in a vision to Daniel thy brother.
text.KJV: 12:11 The Eagle whom thou sawest come vp from the sea, is the kingdome which was seene, in the vision of thy brother Daniel.
text.NRSV: The eagle that you saw coming up from the sea is the fourth kingdom that appeared in a vision to your brother Daniel.

text.DRB: But it was not interpreted to him, therefore I do now interpret it to thee.
text.KJV: 12:12 But it was not expounded vnto him, therefore now I declare it vnto thee.
text.NRSV: But it was not explained to him as I now explain to you or have explained it.

text.DRB: Behold the days come, and there shall rise a Kingdom upon the earth, and the fear shall be more terrible than of all the Kingdoms that were before it.
text.KJV: 12:13 Behold, the dayes will come, that there shall rise vp a kingdome vpon earth, and it shall be feared aboue all the kingdomes that were before it.
text.NRSV: The days are coming when a kingdom shall rise on earth, and it shall be more terrifying than all the kingdoms that have been before it.

text.DRB: And there shall twelve kings reign in it, one after another.
text.KJV: 12:14 In the same shall twelue kings reigne, one after another.
text.NRSV: And twelve kings shall reign in it, one after another.

text.DRB: For the second shall begin to reign, and he shall continue more time than the rest of the twelve.
text.KJV: 12:15 Whereof the second shall begin to reigne, and shall haue more time then any of the twelue.
text.NRSV: But the second that is to reign shall hold sway for a longer time than any other one of the twelve.

text.DRB: And the voice that spake which thou heardest, not coming forth of her heads, but from the midst of her body,
text.KJV: 12:17 As for the voice which thou heardest speake, and that thou sawest not to goe out from the heads, but from the mids of the body thereof, this is the interpretation:
text.NRSV: ‘As for your hearing a voice that spoke, coming not from the eagle’s] heads but from the midst of its body, this is the interpretation:

text.DRB: this is the interpretation, that after the time of that Kingdom shall rise no small contentions, and it shall be in danger to fall: and it shall not fall then, but shall be constituted again according to the beginning thereof.
text.KJV: 12:18 That after the time of that kingdome, there shall arise great striuings, and it shall stand in perill of falling: neuerthelesse it shall not then fall, but shal be restored againe to his beginning.
