text.DRB: The sword is sent in upon you, and who is he that can turn it away?
text.KJV: 16:3 A sword is sent vpon you, and who may turne it backe?
text.NRSV: The sword has been sent upon you, and who is there to turn it back?

text.DRB: Fire is sent in upon you, and who is he that can quench it?
text.KJV: 16:4 A fire is sent among you, and who may quench it?
text.NRSV: A fire has been sent upon you, and who is there to quench it?

text.DRB: Evils are sent in upon you, and who is he that can repel them?
text.KJV: 16:5 Plagues are sent vnto you, and what is he that may driue them away?
text.NRSV: Calamities have been sent upon you, and who is there to drive them away?

text.DRB: Shall any man repel the lion being hungry in the wood, or quench the fire in stubble, forthwith when it beginneth to burn?
text.KJV: 16:6 May any man driue away a hungry Lion in the wood? or may any one quench the fire in stubble, when it hath begun to burne?
text.NRSV: Can one drive off a hungry lion in the forest, or quench a fire in the stubble once it has started to burn?]

text.DRB: Shall any man repel the arrow shot of a strong archer?
text.KJV: 16:7 May one turne againe the arrow that is shot of a strong archer?
text.NRSV: Can one turn back an arrow shot by a strong archer?

text.DRB: Our strong Lord sendeth in evils, & who is he that can repel them?
text.KJV: 16:8 The mightie Lord sendeth the plagues, and who is hee that can driue them away?
text.NRSV: The Lord God sends calamities, and who will drive them away?

text.DRB: Fire came forth from his wrath, and who is he that can quench it?
text.KJV: 16:9 A fire shall goe foorth from his wrath: & who is he that may quench it?
text.NRSV: Fire will go forth from his wrath, and who is there to quench it?

text.DRB: He will lighten, who shall not fear, he will thunder, and who shall not be afraid?
text.KJV: 16:10 He shall cast lightnings, and who shall not feare? he shall thunder, and who shall not be afraid?
text.NRSV: He will flash lightning, and who will not be afraid? He will thunder, and who will not be terrified?

text.DRB: Our Lord will threaten, and who shall not utterly be destroyed before his face?
text.KJV: 16:11 The Lord shall threaten, and who shall not be vtterly beaten to powder at his presence?
text.NRSV: The Lord will threaten, and who will not be utterly shattered at his presence?

text.DRB: The earth hath trembled, and the foundations thereof, the sea tosseth up waves from the depth, and the floods of it shall be destroyed, and the fishes thereof at the face of our Lord, and at the glory of his power:
text.KJV: 16:12 The earth quaketh and the foundations thereof, the sea ariseth vp with waues from the deepe, and the waues of it are troubled, and the fishes thereof also before the Lord, and before the glorie of his power.
text.NRSV: The earth and its foundations quake, the sea is churned up from the depths, and its waves and the fish with them shall be troubled at the presence of the Lord and the glory of his power.

text.DRB: because his right hand is strong which bendeth the bow, his arrows be sharp that are shot of him, they shall not miss, when they shall begin to be shot into the ends of the earth.
text.KJV: 16:13 For strong is his right hand that bendeth the bow, his arrowes that hee shooteth are sharpe, and shall not misse when they begin to bee shot into the ends of the world.
text.NRSV: For his right hand that bends the bow is strong, and his arrows that he shoots are sharp and when they are shot to the ends of the world will not miss once.

text.DRB: Behold evils are sent, and they shall not return till they come upon the earth.
text.KJV: 16:14 Behold, the plagues are sent, and shall not returne againe, vntill they come vpon the earth.
text.NRSV: Calamities are sent forth and shall not return until they come over the earth.

text.DRB: The fire is kindled and it shall not be quenched, till it consume the foundations of the earth.
text.KJV: 16:15 The fire is kindled, and shall not be put out, till it consume the foundation of the earth.
text.NRSV: The fire is kindled, and shall not be put out until it consumes the foundations of the earth.

text.DRB: For as the arrow shot of a strong archer returneth not, so shall not the evils return back, that shall be sent upon the earth.
text.KJV: 16:16 Like as an arrow which is shot of a mightie archer returneth not backward: euen so the plagues that shall be sent vpon earth, shall not returne againe.
text.NRSV: Just as an arrow shot by a mighty archer does not return, so the calamities that are sent upon the earth shall not return.

text.DRB: Woe is me: woe is me: who shall deliver me in those days?
text.KJV: 16:17 Woe is me, woe is me, who will deliuer me in those dayes?
text.NRSV: Alas for me! Alas for me! Who will deliver me in those days?

text.DRB: The beginning of sorrows and much mourning, the beginning of famine and much destruction. The beginning of wars and the potestates shall fear, the beginning of evils and all shall tremble.
text.KJV: 16:18 The beginning of sorrowes, and great mournings, the beginning of famine, and great death: the beginning of warres, and the powers shall stand in feare, the beginning of euils, what shall I doe when these euils shal come?
text.NRSV: The beginning of sorrows, when there shall be much lamentation; the beginning of famine, when many shall perish; the beginning of wars, when the powers shall be terrified; the beginning of calamities, when all shall tremble. What shall they do, when the calamities come?

text.DRB: In these what shall I do, when the evils shall come?
text.KJV: 16:19 Behold, famine, and plague, tribulation and anguish, are sent as scourges for amendment.
text.NRSV: Famine and plague, tribulation and anguish are sent as scourges for the correction of humankind.

text.DRB: Behold famine, and plague, & tribulation, and distress are sent all as scourges for amendment,
text.KJV: 16:20 But for all these things they shall not turne from their wickednes, nor be alway mindfull of the scourges.
text.NRSV: Yet for all this they will not turn from their iniquities, or ever be mindful of the scourges.

text.DRB: and in all these they will not convert themselves from their iniquities, neither will they be always mindful of the scourges.
text.KJV: 16:21 Behold, victuals shall be so good cheape vpon earth, that they shal think themselues to be in good case, and euen then shall euils growe vpon earth, sword, famine, and great confusion.
text.NRSV: Indeed, provisions will be so cheap upon earth that people will imagine that peace is assured for them, and then calamities shall spring up on the earth—the sword, famine, and great confusion.

text.DRB: Behold, there shall be good cheap victuals upon the earth, so that they may think that peace is directly coming toward them, & then shall evils spring upon the earth, sword, famine, and great confusion.
text.KJV: 16:22 For many of them that dwell vpon earth, shall perish of famine, and the other that escape the hunger, shall the sword destroy.
text.NRSV: For many of those who live on the earth shall perish by famine; and those who survive the famine shall die by the sword.

text.DRB: For by famine many that inhabit the earth shall die, and the sword shall destroy the rest that remained alive of the famine,
text.KJV: 16:23 And the dead shall be cast out as doung, and there shalbe no man to comfort them, for the earth shall be wasted, and the cities shall be cast downe.
text.NRSV: And the dead shall be thrown out like dung, and there shall be no one to console them; for the earth shall be left desolate, and its cities shall be demolished.

text.DRB: and the dead shall be cast forth as dung, and there shall be none to comfort them. For the earth shall be left desert, and the cities thereof shall be thrown down.
text.KJV: 16:24 There shall be no man left to till the earth, and to sow it.
text.NRSV: No one shall be left to cultivate the earth or to sow it.

text.DRB: There shall not be left a man to till the ground and to sow it.
text.KJV: 16:25 The trees shall giue fruite, and who shall gather them?
text.NRSV: The trees shall bear fruit, but who will gather it?

text.DRB: The trees shall yield fruits, and who shall gather them?
text.KJV: 16:26 The grapes shall ripe, and who shall treade them? for all places shall be desolate of men.
text.NRSV: The grapes shall ripen, but who will tread them? For in all places there shall be great solitude;

text.DRB: The grape shall become ripe, and who shall tread it? For there shall be great desolation to
text.KJV: 16:27 So that one man shall desire to see another, and to heare his voyce.
text.NRSV: a person will long to see another human being, or even to hear a human voice.

text.DRB: For a man shall desire to see a man, or to hear his voice.
text.KJV: 16:28 For of a citie there shalbe ten left, and two of the field which shall hide themselues in the thicke groues, and in the clefts of rockes.
text.NRSV: For ten shall be left out of a city; and two, out of the field, those who have hidden themselves in thick groves and clefts in the rocks.

text.DRB: For there shall be left ten of a city, and two of the field that have hid themselves in thick woods, and cliffs of rocks.
text.KJV: 16:29 As in an orchard of oliues, vpon euery tree there are left three or foure oliues:
text.NRSV: Just as in an olive orchard three or four olives may be left on every tree,

text.DRB: As there are left in the olivet, and on every tree, three or four olives.
text.KJV: 16:30 Or, when as a vineyard is gathered, there are left some clusters of them that diligently seek through þ vineyard:
text.NRSV: or just as, when a vineyard is gathered, some clusters may be left] by those who search carefully through the vineyard,

text.DRB: Or as in a vineyard when it is gathered there are grapes left by them, that diligently search the vineyard:
text.KJV: 16:31 Euen so in those dayes there shalbe three or foure left by them that search their houses with the sword.
text.NRSV: so in those days three or four shall be left by those who search their houses with the sword.

text.DRB: so shall there be left in those days three or four, by them that search their houses in the sword.
text.KJV: 16:32 And the earth shall be laid waste, and the fields therof shal waxe old, and her wayes and all her paths shall grow full of thornes, because no man shal trauaile therethrough.
text.NRSV: The earth shall be left desolate, and its fields shall be ploughed up,] and its roads and all its paths shall bring forth thorns, because no sheep will go along them.

text.DRB: And the earth shall be left desolate, and the fields thereof shall wax old, and the ways thereof, and all the paths thereof shall bring forth thorns, because no man shall pass by it.
text.KJV: 16:33 The virgins shall mourne hauing no bridegromes, þ women shal mourne hauing no husbands, their daughters shall mourne hauing no helpers.
text.NRSV: Virgins shall mourn because they have no bridegrooms; women shall mourn because they have no husbands; their daughters shall mourn, because they have no help.

text.DRB: Virgins shall mourn having no bridegrooms, women shall mourn having no husbands, their daughters shall mourn having no help:
text.KJV: 16:34 In the warres shall their bridegromes bee destroyed, and their husbands shall perish of famine.
text.NRSV: Their bridegrooms shall be killed in war, and their husbands shall perish of famine.

text.DRB: Their bridegrooms shall be consumed in battle, and their husbands be destroyed in famine.
text.KJV: 16:35 Heare now these things, and vnderstand them, ye seruants of the Lord.
text.NRSV: Listen now to these things, and understand them, you who are servants of the Lord.

text.DRB: But hear these things, and know them ye servants of our Lord.
text.KJV: 16:36 Behold the word of the Lord, receiue it, beleeue not the gods of whom the Lord spake.
text.NRSV: This is the word of the Lord; receive it and do not disbelieve what the Lord says.]

text.DRB: Behold the word of our Lord, receive it: believe not the gods of whom our Lord speaketh.
text.KJV: 16:37 Behold, the plagues draw nigh, and are not slacke.
text.NRSV: The calamities draw near, and are not delayed.

text.DRB: Behold the evils approach, and slack not.
text.KJV: 16:38 As when a woman with childe in the ninth month bringeth forth her son, within two or three houres of her birth great paines compasse her wombe, which paines, when the child commeth forth, they slacke not a moment,
text.NRSV: Just as a pregnant woman, in the ninth month when the time of her delivery draws near, has great pains around her womb for two or three hours beforehand, but when the child comes forth from the womb, there will not be a moment’s delay,

text.DRB: As a woman with child when she bringeth forth her child in the ninth month, the hour of her deliverance approaching, two or three hours before, pains come about her womb, and the infants coming out of her womb, they will not tarry one moment.
text.KJV: 16:39 Euen so shall not the plagues bee slacke to come vpon the earth, and the world shall mourne, and sorrowes shal come vpon it on euery side.
text.NRSV: so the calamities will not delay in coming upon the earth, and the world will groan, and pains will seize it on every side.

text.DRB: So the evils shall not slack to come forth upon the earth, and the world shall lament, and sorrows shall hold it round about.
text.KJV: 16:40 O my people, Heare my word: make you ready to the battell, and in those euils, be euen as pilgrimes vpon the earth.
text.NRSV: Hear my words, O my people; prepare for battle, and in the midst of the calamities be like strangers on the earth.

text.DRB: Hear the word, my people: prepare yourselves unto the fight, & in the evils so be ye as strangers of the earth.
text.KJV: 16:41 He that selleth let him be as hee that fleeth away: and he that buyeth, as one that will loose.
text.NRSV: Let one who sells be like one who will flee; let one who buys be like one who will lose;

text.DRB: He that selleth as if he should fly, & he that buyeth as he that should lose it.
text.KJV: 16:42 He that occupieth merchandize, as he that had no profit by it: and he that buildeth, as hee that shall not dwell therein.
text.NRSV: let one who does business be like one who will not make a profit; and let one who builds a house be like one who will not live in it;

text.DRB: He that playeth the merchant, as he that should take no fruit: and he that buildeth as he that should not inhabit.
text.KJV: 16:43 He that soweth, as if he should not reape: so also he that planteth the vineyard, as he that shal not gather the grapes.
text.NRSV: let one who sows be like one who will not reap; so also one who prunes the vines, like one who will not gather the grapes;

text.DRB: He that soweth, as he that shall not reap: so he also that pruneth a vineyard, as if he should not have the vintage.
text.KJV: 16:44 They that marry, as they that shall get no children: and they that marrie not, as the widowers.
text.NRSV: those who marry, like those who will have no children; and those who do not marry, like those who are widowed.

text.DRB: They that marry so as if they should not get children, and they that marry not, so as it were widows.
text.KJV: 16:45 And therefore they that labour, labour in vaine.
text.NRSV: Because of this, those who labour, labour in vain;

text.DRB: Wherefore they that labour, labour without cause:
text.KJV: 16:46 For strangers shall reape their fruits, and spoile their goods, ouerthrowe their houses; and take their children captiues, for in captiuity and famine shall they get children.
text.NRSV: for strangers shall gather their fruits, and plunder their goods, overthrow their houses, and take their children captive; for in captivity and famine they will produce their children.]

text.DRB: for foreigners shall reap their fruits, and shall violently take their goods, and overthrow their houses, and lead their children captive, because in captivity, and famine they beget their children.
text.KJV: 16:47 And they that occupy their merchandize with robbery, the more they decke their citties, their houses, their possessions and their owne persons:
text.NRSV: Those who conduct business, do so only to have it plundered; the more they adorn their cities, their houses and possessions, and their persons,

text.DRB: And they that play the merchants by robbery, the longer they adorn their cities and houses, and their possessions and persons:
text.KJV: 16:48 The more will I be angry with them for their sinne, saith the Lord.
text.NRSV: the more angry I will be with them for their sins, says the Lord.

text.DRB: so much the more will I be zealous toward them, upon their sins, saith our Lord.
text.KJV: 16:49 Like as an whore enuieth a right honest and vertuous woman:
text.NRSV: Just as a respectable and virtuous woman abhors a prostitute,

text.DRB: As a whore envieth an honest and very good woman:
text.KJV: 16:50 So shall righteousnesse haue iniquity, when she decketh herselfe, and shall accuse her, to her face, when he commeth that shall defend him that diligently searcheth out euery sinne vpon earth.
text.NRSV: so righteousness shall abhor iniquity, when she decks herself out, and shall accuse her to her face when he comes who will defend the one who searches out every sin on earth.

text.DRB: so shall justice hate impiety when she adorneth herself, and accuseth her to her face, when he shall come that may defend him that searcheth out all upon the earth.
text.KJV: 16:51 And therfore be yee not like therunto, nor to the workes thereof.
text.NRSV: Therefore do not be like her or her works.

text.DRB: Therefore be not made like to her, nor to her works.
text.KJV: 16:52 For yet a little iniquitie shall be taken away out of the earth, and righteousnesse shall reigne among you.
text.NRSV: For in a very short time iniquity will be removed from the earth, and righteousness will reign over us.

text.DRB: For yet a little while and iniquity shall be taken away from the earth, and justice shall reign over you.
text.KJV: 16:53 Let not the sinner say that he hath not sinned: for God shall burne coales of fire vpon his head, which saith before the Lord God and his glory, I haue not sinned.
text.NRSV: Sinners must not say that they have not sinned;] for God] will burn coals of fire on the head of everyone who says, ‘I have not sinned before God and his glory.’

text.DRB: Let not the sinner say he hath not sinned: because he shall burn coals of fire upon his head, that saith I have not sinned before our Lord God and his glory.
text.KJV: 16:54 Behold, the Lord knoweth all the workes of men, their imaginations, their thoughts, and their hearts:
text.NRSV: The Lord] certainly knows everything that people do; he knows their imaginations and their thoughts and their hearts.

text.DRB: Behold our Lord shall know all the works of men, and their inventions, and their cogitations, and their hearts. . Luc. 16. .
text.KJV: 16:55 Which spake but the word, let the earth be made, and it was made: let the heauen be made, and it was created.
text.NRSV: He said, ‘Let the earth be made’, and it was made, and ‘Let the heaven be made’, and it was made.

text.DRB: For he said: Let the earth be made, and it was made: let the heaven be made, and it was made.
text.KJV: 16:56 In his word were the starres made, and he knoweth the number of them.
text.NRSV: At his word the stars were fixed in their places, and he knows the number of the stars.

text.DRB: And by his word the stars were made, and he knoweth the number of the stars. . v. 4. Job. 38.
text.KJV: 16:57 He searcheth the deepe, and the treasures thereof, he hath measured the Sea, and what it containeth.
text.NRSV: He searches the abyss and its treasures; he has measured the sea and its contents;

text.DRB: Who searcheth the depth and the treasures thereof: who hath measured the sea, and capacity thereof.
text.KJV: 16:58 He hath shut the Sea in the midst of the waters, and with his word hath he hanged the earth vpon the waters.
text.NRSV: he has confined the sea in the midst of the waters;] and by his word he has suspended the earth over the water.

text.DRB: Who hath shut up the sea in the midst of waters, and hath hanged the earth upon the waters with his word.
text.KJV: 16:59 He spreadeth out the heauens like a vault, vpon the waters hath he founded it.
text.NRSV: He has spread out the heaven like a dome and made it secure upon the waters;

text.DRB: Who hath spread heaven as it were a vault, over the waters he hath founded it.
text.KJV: 16:60 In the desart hath hee made springs of water, and pooles vpon the tops of the mountaines, that the floods might powre downe from the high rockes to water the earth.
text.NRSV: he has put springs of water in the desert, and pools on the tops of the mountains, so as to send rivers from the heights to water the earth.

text.DRB: Who hath put fountains of waters in the desert, and lakes upon the tops of mountains, to send forth rivers from the high rock to water the earth.
text.KJV: 16:61 He made man, and put his heart in the midst of the body, and gaue him breath, life, and vnderstanding.
text.NRSV: He formed human beings and put a heart in the midst of each body, and gave each person breath and life and understanding

text.DRB: Who made man and put his heart in the midst of the body, and gave him spirit, life, and understanding.
text.KJV: 16:62 Yea and the spirit of Almighty God, which made all things, and searcheth out all hidden things in the secrets of the earth.
text.NRSV: and the spirit] of Almighty God,] who surely made all things and searches out hidden things in hidden places.

text.DRB: And the inspiration of God omnipotent that made all things, and searcheth all hid things, in the secrets of the earth.
text.KJV: 16:63 Surely he knoweth your inuentions, and what you thinke in your hearts, euen them that sinne, and would hide their sinne.
text.NRSV: He knows your imaginations and what you think in your hearts! Woe to those who sin and want to hide their sins!

text.DRB: He knoweth your invention, and what you think in your hearts sinning, and willing to hide your sins.
text.KJV: 16:64 Therefore hath the Lord exactly searched out all your workes, and he will put you all to shame.
text.NRSV: The Lord will strictly examine all their works, and will make a public spectacle of all of you.

text.DRB: Wherefore our Lord in searching hath searched all your works, and he will put you all to open shame,
text.KJV: 16:65 And when your sinnes are brought foorth yee shalbe ashamed before men, and your owne sinnes shall be your accusers in that day.
text.NRSV: You shall be put to shame when your sins come out before others, and your own iniquities shall stand as your accusers on that day.

text.DRB: and you shall be confounded when your sins shall
text.KJV: 16:66 What will yee doe? or how will yee hide your sinnes before God and his Angels?
text.NRSV: What will you do? Or how will you hide your sins before the Lord and his glory?

text.KJV: 16:67 Behold, God himselfe is the iudge, feare him: leaue off from your sinnes, and forget your iniquities to medle no more with them for euer, so shall God lead you forth, and deliuer you from all trouble.
text.NRSV: Behold, God is the judge; fear him! Do not sin any more, and forget your iniquities, to never commit them again; so God will lead you forth and deliver you from all tribulation.
text.DRB: What will you do? Or how shall you hide your sins before God and his Angels?
Behold God is the Judge, fear him. Cease from your sins, and now forget your iniquities to do them any more, and God will bring you out, and deliver you from all tribulation.
text.KJV: 16:68 For behold, the burning wrath of a great multitude is kindled ouer you, and they shall take away certaine of you, and feede you being idle with things offered vnto idoles.
text.NRSV: The burning wrath of a great multitude is kindled over you; they shall drag some of you away and force you to eat what was sacrificed to idols.

text.DRB: For behold the heat of a great multitude is kindled over you, and they shall take certain of you by violence, and shall make the slain to be meat for idols.
text.KJV: 16:69 And they that consent vnto them shall be had in derision, and in reproch, and troden vnder foote.
text.NRSV: And those who consent to eat shall be held in derision and contempt, and shall be trampled under foot.

text.DRB: And they that shall consent unto them, shall be to them in derision, and in reproach, and in conculcation.
text.KJV: 16:70 For there shall be in euery place, and in the next cities a great insurrection vpon those that feare the Lord.
text.NRSV: For in many places] and in neighbouring cities there shall be a great uprising against those who fear the Lord.

text.DRB: for there shall be place against places, and against the next cities great insurrection upon them that fear our Lord.
text.KJV: 16:71 They shall be like mad men, sparing none, but still spoiling and destroying those that feare the Lord.
text.NRSV: They shall] be like maniacs, sparing no one, but plundering and destroying those who continue to fear the Lord.]

text.DRB: They shall be as it were mad sparing nobody, to spoil and waste yet them that fear our Lord.
text.KJV: 16:72 For they shal waste and take away their goods, and cast them out of their houses.
text.NRSV: For they shall destroy and plunder their goods, and drive them out of house and home.

text.DRB: because they shall waste and spoil the goods, and shall cast them out of their houses.
text.KJV: 16:73 Then shall they be knowen who are my chosen, and they shall be tried, as the gold in the fire:
text.NRSV: Then the tested quality of my elect shall be manifest, like gold that is tested by fire.

text.DRB: Then, shall appear the probation of mine elect, as gold that is proved by the fire.
text.KJV: 16:74 Heare, O yee my beloued, saith the Lord: behold, the dayes of trouble are at hand, but I will deliuer you from the same.
text.NRSV: Listen, my elect ones, says the Lord; the days of tribulation are at hand, but I will deliver you from them.

text.DRB: Hear my beloved, saith our Lord: Behold the days of tribulation are come: and out of them I will deliver you.
text.KJV: 16:75 Be yee not afraid, neither doubt, for God is your guide,
text.NRSV: Do not fear or doubt, for God] is your guide.

text.DRB: Do not fear, nor stagger, because God is your guide.
text.KJV: 16:76 And the guide of them who keepe my commaundements, and precepts, saith the Lord God; Let not your sinnes weigh you downe, and let not your iniquities lift vp themselues.
text.NRSV: You who keep my commandments and precepts, says the Lord God, must not let your sins weigh you down, or your iniquities prevail over you.

text.DRB: And he that keepeth my commandments, and precepts, saith our Lord God: Let not your sins overweigh you, nor your iniquities be advanced over you.
text.KJV: 16:77 Woe bee vnto them that are bound with their sinnes, and couered with their iniquities: like as a field is couered ouer with bushes, and the path thereof couered with thornes, that no man may trauell through.
text.NRSV: Woe to those who are choked by their sins and overwhelmed by their iniquities! They are like a field choked with underbrush and its path] overwhelmed with thorns, so that no one can pass through.

text.KJV: 16:78 It is left vndressed, and is cast into the fire, to bee consumed therewith.
text.NRSV: Woe to those who are bound by their sins and overwhelmed by their iniquities, as a field which is choked with undergrowth and its path overwhelmed with thorns, so that no one can pass through! It is shut off and given up to be consumed by fire.
text.DRB: Woe to them that are entangled with their sins, and are covered with their iniquities, as a field is entangled with the wood, and the path thereof covered with thorns, by which no man passeth, and it is closed out, and cast to be devoured of the fire.
